ALEXANDER, CHARLES LESLIE, Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Shrewsbury and Trin. Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1897; arrived, 7th Dec., 1898, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr.

ALEXANDER, EDWIN JOHN, Indian Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at Cooper's Hill Coll.; apptd. to the Dept. as assist. engr., Sept., 1880, and posted to State Railways; exec. engr., Aug., 1893.

ALEXANDER, ERNEST BRUCE, Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Repton; apptd. after exam. of 1869; arrived, 1st Nov., 1871, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr., asst. settl. officer, junr. sec. to the board of rev., joint mag. and dep. collr., and dep. commr.; mag. and collr., Oct., 1889; in 1894 acted as sec. to govt., jud. dept.; commr., Nov., 1896; author of 'Gazetteer of Gorakhpur.'

ALEXANDER, JAMES WILLIAM, late India Public Works Dept.—Joined the service, June, 1865; asst. engr., Dec., 1866; asst. sec. to govt. of India, public works dept., Jan., 1868; exec. engr., March, 1870; suptg. engr., Feb., 1890; ret'd., Mar., 1899.

ALEXANDER, MOSES (Second class clerk, Store

Dept., India Office).—Clerk, 2nd class, 3rd March, 1888.

ALEXANDER, NATHANIEL STUART, late Bengal C.S.—Apptd. from Haileybury Coll.; arrived, 10th July, 1858; from Oct., 1859, served in the Bengal rev. and jud. depts., and was apptd. mag. and collr., 1st grade, Jan., 1879; commr., Dacca, Aug., 1884; Bhagalpur, Dec., 1885; and Bardwan, Aug., 1887; ret'd., March, 1890.

ALGIE, WILLIAM, B.A., B.C.E., India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Apptd. apprentice engr., 19th April, 1875, and posted to Burma; asst. engr., April, 1876; exec. engr., May, 1885; employed on irrig. works in Egypt, 1885-86.

ALI AHMAD HASSANALLY, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Karachi Mohammedan Sch., Bombay Univ., and Queen's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1894; arrived 27th Feb., 1896, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr. and asst. commr.

ALISON, SIR ARCHIBALD, Bart., G.C.B., Gen., late member of Council of India.—First commn., ensign, 72nd foot, 3rd Nov., 1846; served in the Crimean campaign of 1855, and was present at siege of Sevastopol (medal and clasp and Turkish medal); served in the Indian Mutiny, and was mil. sec. to the commander-in-chief, Aug., 1857, to March, 1858; was present at relief of Lucknow (arm lost, medal and clasp, and C.B.); in staff employment, adjutant-gen.'s dept. at headquarters, and in the south western district, and at Aldershot, 1862-73; served in the Ashanti war of 1873-74 as brigadier-gen. of European brigade (thanks of both houses of parliament, medal with clasp, and K.C.B.); dep. adjutant-gen., headquarters, Ireland, 1874-77; commandant, staff college, 1878; dep. quartermaster-gen., intelligence branch, 1878-82; served in the Egyptian expdn. of 1882 as major-gen. in command of troops at Alexandria, and on the Mahmoudiyeh canal, and, in command of 3rd brigade, 2nd divn., at battle of Tel-el-Kebir (thanks of both houses of parliament, medal with clasp, bronze star, and 2nd class Osmanieh); promoted lieut.-gen. for distinguished service, Nov., 1882; commanding divn., Aldershot, Aug., 1883, to Feb., 1885; and again, Oct., 1885; adjutant-gen. to the forces, Feb.-Sept., 1885; G.C.B., June, 1887; member of the council of India, Jan., 1889 to Jan., 1899.

ALLEN, BASIL COPLESTON, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Assam).—Educ. at Haileybury and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1892; arrived, 14th Dec., 1893, and served in Assam as asst. commr. and personal asst. to chief commr.

ALLEN, BERNARD, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr. Bengal).—Educ. at Lancing Coll. and Trin. Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1894, arrived 21st Dec., 1895, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

ALLEN, CHARLES FRANCIS EGERTON, B.A.; B.L., late Jud. Dept., Burma.—Called to the bar (Inner Temple), 1871; admitted advocate of high court, Calcutta, Nov., 1873; law exam., Calcutta univ., Dec., 1874, and again in 1875 and 1876; acting judge of the Calcutta court of small causes, 1875-76; lecturer on English law, presidency coll., Calcutta, Feb., 1876, and profir. of law (temporary), May, 1877; served as govt. advocate, Rangoon, and acted as recorder of

Rangoon for various periods; M.P. for Pembroke district, 1892-95.

ALLEN, CHARLES GEORGE HILLERSDEN, Indian C.S. (mag. and collr. Bengal).—Educ. at Haileybury and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1883; arrived, 16th Dec., 1885, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and coll. and settl. officer; joint mag. and dep. collr., Jan., 1893; mag. and collr., Nov., 1896.

ALLEN, DAVID BIRD, late Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Eton, and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1871; arrived, 5th Nov., 1873, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., asst. to dir.-gen. of statistics, and under the dir. of the agricl. dept., May, 1885; joint mag. and dep. collr., Sept., 1887; mag. and collr., Aug., 1890; ret'd., Jan., 1899.

ALLEN, EDWARD CUTHBERT, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at the Charterhouse and Lincoln Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1894; arrived, 6th Dec., 1895, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.

ALLEN, JEFFERY JULIAN, M.I.E.C. (dir. traffic branch, India Telegraph Dept.).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 26th Nov., 1868; supt., Aug., 1881; dir. traffic branch, May, 1895.

ALLEN, PERCY UNDERHILL, B.A., Indian C.S. (dep. commr. N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Univ. Coll. Sch., Cheltenham Coll., and Magdalen Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1885; arrived, 21st Nov., 1888, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., April, 1892; dep. commr., May, 1898.

ALLEN, THOMAS TAYLOR, late Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Queen's Coll., Cork; apptd. after exam. of 1859; arrived, 7th Feb., 1861, and served in the Bengal rev. and jud. depts.; joint mag. and dep. collr., April, 1866; district and sessions judge, March, 1875; supt. and remembrancer of legal affairs, Feb., 1883; served for various periods as member of legisl. council; ret'd., Nov., 1894.

ALLEN, WALTER GRIFFITH, India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at Cooper's Hill Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1874, and employed on state railways; exec. engr., May, 1883.

ALLEN, WILLIAM, late Bombay C.S.—Educ. at King's Coll. Sch., and St. Catherine's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1866; arrived, 10th Oct., 1868, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; junr. collr. and mag., Nov., 1889; senr. collr. and mag., Nov., 1891; ret'd., Sept., 1893.

ALLISON, HAZLETT, M.D., Lieut.-Col., Indian Med. Service (Profr. Med. Coll., Madras).—Joined the service as surgn., Sept., 1873; arrived, 29th April, 1874, and served on mil. duty; fort surgn., Fort St. George, profr. of anatomy in the med. coll., and med. inspr. of seamen, Jan., 1888; fellow of Madras univ., Aug., 1892.

ALLSOP, JAMES GEORGE WHITTLESEA, late Punjab Police.—Joined the police as asst. dist. supt., Nov. 1860; dist. supt., July, 1880.

AMBICA CHARAN SEN, M.A., Statutory C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Bengal).—Apptd. under the native civil service rules asst. mag. and collr., 21st Jan., 1884; confd., 19th Feb., 1886; joint mag. and dep. collr., Jan., 1893; mag. and collr., Feb., 1897; dist. and sess. judge, Feb., 1898.

AMEER ALI, SYED, C.I.E., M.A., B.L. (Judge,

Calcutta High Court).—Educ. at Calcutta Univ.; called to the bar (Inner Temple), 1873; presidency and chief presidency mag., 1878-81; member of the Bengal legisl. council, 1878-79, and 1881-83; member of the supreme legisl. council, 1884-85; Tagore law profr., 1884; C.I.E., 1887; judge of the high court at Calcutta, April, 1890; author of the 'Tagore Law Lectures for 1884.'

AMEER HOSSEIN, THE HON. SYED, C.I.E., Nawab, Bahadur (presy. mag., Calcutta).—Joined the service as offg. dep. mag. and dep. collr., 29th Aug., 1864; confd., 5th Feb., 1867; member of Bengal legisl. council, April, 1878; member of central exam. committee, April, 1886; addl. member of govr.-gen.'s council, Dec., 1886, and again Dec., 1888; C.I.E., Jan., 1888; presidency mag., Calcutta, Nov., 1888; acted as coroner of Calcutta in 1888-89 and in 1896; and as inspr.-gen. of registrn. in 1893 and 1894.

AMIRTA LAL CHATTERJEE, B.L., late Bengal Jud. Dept.—From 19th May, 1862, served in the subord. jud. service, Bengal, subord. judge, April, 1873 to 1896.

ANDERSON, ALEXANDER, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at Gordon's Hosp., New Gr. Sch., and King's Coll., Aberdeen; apptd. after exam. of 1870; arrived, 4th Nov., 1872, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr., and forest settl. officer; dep. commr., March, 1889; C.I.E., Jan., 1897; offg. comm., Oct., 1898.

ANDERSON, FRANCIS CHARLES, late Indian C.S. (Central Prov.).—Educ. at Edinburgh Acad. and Repton Gr. Sch.; apptd. after exam. of 1869; arrived, 31st Oct., 1871, and served in the Central Prov. as asst. commr., personal asst., and asst. sec. to the chief commr.; commr. of excise supt. of stamps, and inspr.-gen. of registrn.; Feb., 1882; sec. to chief commr., Jan., 1891; commr., Jabalpur, April, 1894; ret'd., Jan., 1898; prepared an excise manual for the Central Prov., 1885.

ANDERSON, FRANCIS GEOFFREY HARTNELL, Indian C.S. (asst. comptr., Central Prov.).—Educ. at Bath Coll. and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1892; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1893, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; asst. pol. agent, Savanur, June, 1896; asst. comptr., Central Prov., Nov., 1899.

ANDERSON, GEORGE ARTHUR, India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at Cooper's Hill Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1875, and posted to Central India; transfed. to state railways, Jan., 1878; exec. engr., Oct., 1885; offg. dep. consulting engr. for railways, Bombay, June, 1886; services lent to Bengal-Nagpur railway-co., Nov., 1887; undersec. to Govt. P.W.D., Oct., 1896; offg. sec. to govt. railway branch, Bombay, March, 1898.

ANDERSON, HENRY AIKEN, C.S.I., Indian C.S. (commr. and supt., Punjab).—Educ. at Edinburgh Acad. and Repton Gr. Sch.; apptd. after exam. of 1871; arrived, 13th Nov., 1873, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; asst. pol. officer, Kuram, Aug., 1880; dep. commr., May, 1889; acted as commr. and supt., Derajat, in 1892, also in 1895 and 1896; on special duty with Waziristan delimitation commn. in 1894, and from Feb., 1895; C.S.I., June, 1897; commr. and supt., March, 1899.

ANDERSON, JAMES, M.A., B.L., late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at Edinburgh Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1860; arrived, 14th Nov., 1861; served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., joint mag.,

dep. commr., and mag. and collr.; on special duty connected with the Wahabi case, June, 1870; ret'd., 1888.

ANDERSON, JAMES ARTHUR, M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Joined the dept. as asst. engr., 13th Dec., 1871; exec. engr., Dec., 1876; employed on the Sind-Pishin, Indus valley and E. Bengal railways; engr.-in-chief, Kashmir railway survey, Oct., 1890; supt., way and works, E. Bengal railway, July, 1892; suptg. engr., Oct., 1892; chief engr., March, 1896; ret'd., 1899.

ANDERSON, JAMES DRUMMOND, Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Rugby; apptd. after exam. of 1873; arrived, 14th Nov., 1875, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transf. to Assam, Jan., 1880, and served as asst. commr., and asst. sec. to chief commr.; dep. commr., April, 1889; returned to Bengal as mag. and collr., April, 1894; author of vocabularies of the Tippera-Deori-Chutia and Aka languages, and a collection of Kachari folk stories.

ANDERSON, JOHN ALEXANDER, B.L., Indian C.S. (divl. judge, Punjab).—Educ. at the Gr. Sch. and Univ. of Aberdeen, and was also a student of the Inner Temple; apptd. after exam. of 1872; arrived, 2nd Dec., 1874, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; district judge, April, 1889; divnl. judge, June, 1894.

ANDERSON, JOHN WATSON MACCLESFIELD, Col., late Rev. Survey Dept., Bombay.—First commn., Jan., 1855; from Nov., 1863, served in the rev. survey dept., Mysore, as asst. supt. and dep. supt.; from April, 1881, served as supt. of the Sind settlt. survey; ret'd., March, 1888.

ANDERSON, ROBERT HILDEBRAND, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Bengal).—Apptd. after exam. of 1874; arrived, 7th Dec., 1876, and served as asst. mag. and collr., and joint mag. and dep. collr.; district and sessions judge, Oct., 1893; acted as jud. commr., Chota Nagpur, in 1895.

ANDREW, JAMES, Indian C.S. (collr. and mag., Madras).—Educ. at the Royal Sch., Armagh, and Kingstown Sch.; apptd. after exam. of 1877; arrived, 17th Nov., 1879, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag., and forest settlt. officer; head asst. to collr. and mag., N. Arcot, April, 1888; India principal asst., Ganjam, Dec., 1891; collr. and mag., Trichinopoli, Nov., 1895; collr. and mag., and govt. agent, Tanjore, Oct., 1897.

ANDREWS, WILLIAM (Second Class Clerk, Accint.-Gen.'s Dept., India Office).—Clerk, 2nd class, 30th March, 1876.

ANLEY, GEORGE AUGUSTUS D'AUVERGNE, late Public Works Dept., Bengal.—Apptd. exec. engr., 25th Jan., 1861; served in the Bengal public works dept., and temporarily under the municipality of Calcutta; offg. asst. to chief engr. and asst. sec. to the govt. of Bengal, April, 1876; suptg. engr., March, 1882; ret'd., Nov., 1888.

ANNESLEY, ROBERT, Major, late Jud. Dept., N.W. Prov.—Entered the army in 1855, and served in the Crimean and Abyssinian campaigns; ret'd. in 1869; joined the service, 4th Jan., 1871, and served as asst. district supt. of police; cantonmt. mag., June, 1877; judge, small cause court, June, 1880; 1st class cantonmt. mag., Oct., 1886; ret'd., April, 1895.

ANSON, GEORGE WEMYSS, Lieut.-Col., I.S.C. (agent and dep. commr., Oudh).—First commn., 11th Jan., 1867; private sec. and A.D.C. to

lieut.-govr., N.W. Prov., 1871, and 1875-78; from April, 1872, served as offg. cantonmt. mag. and offg. asst. commr.; confd. in latter appt., Sept., 1875, and in the former, Sept., 1877; agent, Balrampur estate, June, 1886; in addn. cantonmt. mag., Aug., 1887; agent and dep. commr., Nov., 1890; acting supt. of Port Blair, Dec., 1897.

ANTHONY, ADAM HENRY, Finl. Dept. Govt. of India (dep. acct.-genl., Burma).—Entered the dept., Oct., 1876; asst. compt. -gen. in charge of paper currency, April, 1892; dep. acct.-genl., Burma, Dec., 1898.

APJOHN, JAMES HENRY, M.A., M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept. (chief engr.).—Joined the service, 22nd Dec., 1868, as asst. engr., and was employed on irrig. works in Bengal; exec. engr., Sept., 1873; chief engr., Mar., 1898; services lent to Calcutta port commrs., Mar., 1887.

APLIN, STEPHEN LUSHINGTON, Capt., I.S.C. (judge, small causes court, Burma).—First Commn., Sept., 1883; joined Burma Commn., April, 1891, and served as asst. commr., cantonment mag., and judge, small causes court.

ARBUTHNOT, SIR ALEXANDER JOHN, K.C.S.I., C.I.E., late Madras C.S., late Member of the Council of India.—Educ. at Rugby and Haileybury Coll., and apptd. to the Madras civil service in 1842; arrived, Sept., 1842; served as asst. collr. and mag., Malayalam translr., sec. to the coll. and univ. boards, and dep. and acting registrar to the sadar court; dir. of public instrn., March, 1855; chief sec. to govt. and member of legisl. council, Oct., 1862; member of Madras council, Oct., 1867, to Oct., 1872; temporary govr. of Madras in the absence of Lord Napier, Feb.-May, 1872; ret'd. from the Madras civil service, Oct., 1874; member of govr.-gen.'s council, June, 1875, to April, 1880, and acted as prest. in 1878 and 1879; vice-chancellor of the univ. of Madras, Oct., 1871, and of the univ. of Calcutta, Oct., 1878; counsellor of the Empress of India, Jan., 1877; member of the council of India, 1st Nov., 1887-31st Oct., 1897; vice-prest., 1893-94; author of various publns. on educ. and other subjects in connection with India, among which are 'Reports of Selected Cases determined in the Court of Foudjari Adawlut,' 1851; 'Papers relating to Public Instruction in Madras,' 1855; 'A Memoir of Sir Thomas Munro,' 1881; &c.

ARBUTHNOT, ROBERT EDWARD VAUGHAN, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma).—Educ. at the Merchant Taylors' Sch., and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1890; arrived, 15th Nov., 1892, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; under sec., Nov., 1897; asst. settlt. officer, 1899.

ARBUTHNOTT, JOHN CAMPBELL, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Assam).—Educ. at Fettes Coll., Edinburgh; apptd. after exam. of 1877; arrived, 21st Dec., 1879, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transf. to Assam, Feb., 1883, and served as asst. commr.; dep. commr., Nov., 1890; C.I.E., Jan., 1898.

ARCHER, CHARLES, Capt., I.S.C., Foreign Dept., Govt. of India (pol. agent, Sinjai).—First commn., 9th Sept., 1882; entered civil employ, 8th Aug., 1887, as acting asst. agent, Baluchistan; confd., May, 1889; asst. sec. to govt. of India, foreign dept., April, 1891; pol. agent in Zhow, Jan., 1894; pol. agent, Sinjai, March, 1899.

ARDAGH, SIR JOHN CHARLES, K.C.I.E., C.B., Maj.-Genl., R.E. (dir. of mil. intelligence, War Office).—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Dublin; entered royal engrs. as lieut., 1st April, 1859; attached to the special embassy during the congress at Berlin, June, 1878, and was made a C.B. (civil), July, 1878; apptd. asst. to commr. for the delimitation of the frontier of Bulgaria, Sept., 1875; commr. for the demarcation of the Greek frontier, June, 1881; served in the Egyptian campaign of 1882-84, including the operations at Alexandria, and the battle of Tel-el-Kebir (medal with clasp, bronze star, and 4th class of Osmanieh); served in the Soudan in 1884, commanding royal engrs. and chief of the intelligence dept. (battles of Teb and Tamai; 2 clasps); C.B. (mil.), May, 1884; served in Soudan expdn., 1884-85; commandant of base at Cairo, and in the expdn. of 1885-86 with the frontier field force; received the 3rd class of the Medjidie; private sec. to the Marquess of Lansdowne, govr.-gen. of India, from Dec., 1888, to Jan., 1894; C.I.E., Jan., 1892; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1894; commandant of sch. of mil. engrg., Chatham, April, 1895 to March, 1896; dir. of mil. intell., War Office, April, 1896.

ARGYLL (DUKE OF), HIS GRACE GEORGE DOUGLAS CAMPBELL, K.G., K.T., P.C.—Born in 1823; succeeded to the dukedom in 1847; chancellor of the univ. of St. Andrew's, 1851; lord privy seal, 1852; postmr.-gen., 1855; K.T., 1856; lord privy seal, 1859; in 1860 he was again apptd. postmr.-gen., and subsequently lord privy seal; sec. of state for India, Dec., 1868, to Feb., 1874; lord privy seal, May, 1880; resigned in April, 1881; K.G., 1884; elected rector of the univ. of Glasgow, Nov., 1854; presided over the Glasgow meeting of the British association, 1855; prest. of the royal society of Edinburgh, 1861; is hereditary master of the Queen's household in Scotland, a trustee of the British museum, and hereditary sheriff and lord lieut. of Argyllshire; has publ. 'The Reign of Law, 'Primeval Man,' 'The Eastern Question from the Treaty of Paris to the Treaty of Berlin, and to the second Afghan War,' &c.

ARMSTRONG, FRANCIS BUSFEILD (senior clerk, finl. dept., India Office).—Educ. at Cheltenham Proprietary Coll., and King Edward's Sch., Birmingham; junr. clerk, finl. dept., 30th March, 1860; senr. clerk, Sept., 1883.

ARMSTRONG, JOHN ARTHUR, Lieut.-Col., R.E., late India Public Works Dept.—First commn., June, 1860; joined the dept., Nov., 1865, and served in the Punjab as asst. engr., and in the Hazara campaign, 1868, in charge of field telegraph; exec. engr., Sept., 1869; asst. to chief engr., and asst. sec. to govt., April, 1877; served in Kashmir on special roadwork, 1881-82; suptg. engr., March, 1886; ret'd., March, 1887.

ARMSTRONG, JOHN SCOBELL, late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at Marlborough Coll.; apptd. after exam. of 1864; arrived, 3rd March, 1866, and served in the rev. and jud. depts. in Bengal; dep. collr. of customs, Calcutta, Dec., 1877; mag. and collr., and collr. of customs, Calcutta, April, 1883; port commr., Calcutta, Nov., 1887; ret'd., July, 1892.

ARNOLD, GEORGE FREDERICK, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. sec. to govt., Burma).—Educ. at Merchant Taylors' Sch., and Queen's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1892; arrived, 1st Jan., 1894, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; asst. sec. to govt., Oct., 1897.

ARNOTT, JAMES, M.D., C.M., Brigade-Surgn.-Lieut.-Col., late Bombay Med. Dept.—Joined the Indian med. service, 1st April, 1867; arrived, 25th Oct., 1867, and was employed under the mil. dept. till Aug., 1868, including service in the Abyssinian expdn. (medal), and subsequently as asst.-surgn. in the David Sassoon hosp., Poona, and in the European gen. hosp., Bombay, and as prof. of pathology in the Grant med. coll.; served in Afghan war of 1879-80 (medal); surgn., European gen. hosp., Bombay, March-April, 1882; prof. of midwifery, Grant med. coll. and obstetric physician, Jamsetji Jijibho hosp., April, 1882; physician, European gen. hosp., May, 1891; awarded a good service pension, 1896; ret'd., April, 1898.

ARNOTT, NAPOLEON, Maj.-Gen., R.E., late Mil. Works Dept., Govt. of India.—First commn., 22nd Dec., 1865; arrived, 13th Dec., 1868, and served in Bengal and the Punjab on irrig. duty; exec. engr., Jan., 1879; offd. as sec. to agent to govr.-gen., Baluchistan, and as sec. to defence committee in 1882; employed on special defence works, Aug., 1887; suptg. engr., Dec., 1889; dep. dir.-gen., mil. works, Sept., 1890; transf. to Madras command as chief engr., Mar., 1894; dir.-gen. of mil. works in India, June, 1896; ret'd., March, 1899.

ARTHUR, SIGISMUND RAYNOR, Indian C.S. (1st asst., Bombay).—Educ. at Charterhouse, and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1886; arrived, 19th Nov., 1888, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and forest settl. officer; 2nd asst., March, 1896; 1st asst., July, 1898.

ARUNDEL, ARUNDEL TAGG, C.S.I., B.A., Indian C.S. (member of council, Madras).—Educ. at Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1864; arrived, 18th Nov., 1865, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; private sec. to govr. of Madras, July, 1875; prest. of the municipal commn., Madras, June, 1881; fellow of the Madras univ., March, 1882; principal asst. to collr. and mag., Vizagapatam, Oct., 1884; district and sessions judge, Kistna, Dec., 1884; collr. and mag., Kistna, Aug., 1887; member, board of rev., and member of legisl. council, Jan., 1894; sec. to govt., rev. dept., Dec., 1896; chief sec. to govt., Madras, and member of legisl. council, Oct., 1897; acting member of council, Madras, June, 1898; confd. Sept., 1898; C.S.I., Dec., 1898.

ARUNDELL, EDWARD WILLIAM, B.A., F.C.H., India Public Works Dept. (suptg. engr.).—Trained at Cooper's Hill Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1874, and employed on surveys and other railway work in the Punjab; exec. engr., May, 1883; dep. consulting engr. to govt. of India for railways, central divn., May, 1887; Lucknow, Nov., 1894; suptg. engr., April, 1897.

ASGHAR ALI, SHEIKH QANUNGO, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at Christ's Coll., Camb., and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived, 16th Dec., 1896, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

ASHBURNER, LIONEL R., C.S.I., late Bombay C.S.—Apptd. from Haileybury, 1848; served in the rev. and jud. depts. in Broach, Surat, and Kaira; on the outbreak of the Mutiny was apptd. supt. of police, Kaira, and organised a body of horse and foot for the protection of the eastern frontier; special commr. for trial of rebels, 1858; collr. and mag., Khadesh, 1861; rev. and police commr., N. divn., 1872; member

of council, Bombay, Aug., 1877; acted as govr., 13th March to 28th April, 1880; retd., 1883.

ASHBY, JOHN SHUCKBURGH, Major, Indian Staff Corps (asst. pol. agent, Kathiawar).—First comm., March, 1876; on mil. duty to 1889; asst. pol. resdt., Aden, Mar., 1889; asst. pol. resdt., Kathiawar, Sept., 1894.

ASHE, ROBERT WILLIAM D'ESTCOURT, Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Madras).—Educ. at Dublin High Sch., at the Royal Univ. of Ireland, and Trin. Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1894; arrived 4th Dec., 1895, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.

ASHTON, GEORGE (second class clerk, Finl. Dept., India Office).—Clerk, 2nd class, 7th April, 1884.

ASHWORTH, ERNEST HORATIO, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Merchant Taylors' Sch. and St. John's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1893; arrived, 11th Dec., 1894, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.

ASTON, HENRY FAURE, B.L., Indian C.S. (agent for sardars in the Deccan).—Educ. at the S. African Coll.; apptd. after exam. of 1867; arrived, 9th Oct., 1869, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., railway mag., and cantonmt. mag.; 1st grade, asst. judge and sessions judge, Aug., 1883; jud. asst., Kathiawar, March, 1885; judge and sessions judge, Nov., 1886; on special duty in connection with settlt. cases, Baroda, in 1888; offg. jud. commr., Lower Burma, April, 1894, and Jan.–May, 1895; agent for sardars in the Deccan, March, 1899.

ATA-ULLAH KHAN, MIRZA, Sardar Bahadur, Hon. Lieut.-Col.—British agent at Kabul, June, 1885; retd., June, 1891.

ATKINS, CHARLES HERBERT, Indian C.S. (registrar, chief court, Punjab).—Educ. at King Henry VIII's Sch., Coventry, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1889; arrived, 30th Nov., 1891, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; registrar, chief court, Feb., 1898.

ATKINS, JOHN DE COURCY, Indian C.S. (sec. to govt., gen. depts., Bombay).—Educ. at Univ. Coll. Sch., London Univ., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1878; arrived, 9th Nov., 1880, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and forest settlt. officer; under sec. to govt. in the fin. and rev. depts.; dep. acctnt.-gen., post mr.-gen.; collr., and dist. mag.; junr. sec. to govt. in the gen. educ., &c., depts., April, 1895; sec. to govt., July, 1896.

ATKINSON, FREDERIC JAMES, Finl. Dept., Govt. of India (dep. acctnt.-gen., Bengal).—Joined the service as prob., 8th July, 1873, and served as district grain acctnt. in Bengal, and as asst. acctnt.-gen., in Bombay, Bengal, and N.W. Prov.; asst. comptr., Burma, Dec., 1888; dep. acctnt.-gen., N.W. Prov. and Oudh, June, 1891; dep. acctnt.-gen., Bengal, March, 1896; author of several works on currency and exchange.

ATKINSON, JOHN NATHANIEL, Indian C.S. (collr. of customs, Madras).—Educ. at Marlborough Coll.; apptd. after exam. of 1876; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1878, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag., and asst. sessions judge; on special duty under the commr. of salt rev., April, 1885; head asst. to collr. and mag., Sept., 1886; sub-collr. and joint mag., Jan., 1891; collr. and mag., Dec., 1894; collr.

of customs, protector of emigrants, supt. of stamps, and chairman, Harbour Trust Board, Madras, Nov., 1897.

ATKINSON, ROBERT PHILIP, A.M.I.C.E., Public Works Dept. (supig. engr.).—Apptd. from Roy. Ind. Engineering Coll., 1st Oct., 1876, as asst. engr., posted to N.W. Prov. and Oudh; exec. engr., Jan., 1885; employed on the Ganges Canal; suptg. engr., May, 1898.

ATLAY, Ven. B. T., M.A., late Ecclesl. Dept., Bengal.—Arrived, 17th Oct., 1867; chaplain of St. Paul's Cathedral and of the gen. hosp., Dec., 1867; senr. chaplain, Oct., 1877; senr. chaplain, St. Paul's Cathedral, and chaplain of the gen. hosp. and presidency jail, Jan., 1882; commissary to the lord bishop, and offg. chaplain in charge of the diocese, March, 1883; archdeacon of Calcutta, Sept., 1883; retd., 1889, and was apptd. vicar of Willesden.

ATUL CHANDRA CHATTERJEE, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., N.W. Provs. and Oudh).—Educ. at Calcutta Univ. and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1866; arrived, 10th Dec., 1897, and served in the N.W.P. as asst. mag. and collr.

ATUL CHUNDER DUTI, Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Madras).—Educ. at the Free Church Instn., Calcutta, and at Christ's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1887; arrived, 14th Jan., 1890, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; head asst., Dec. 1896; asst. collr. and mag., July, 1897.

AUCOTT, ELLIOTT (second class clerk, Funds Dept., India Office).—Clerk, 2nd class, 27th Feb., 1886.

AULAD HUSSAIN, C.I.E., Khan Bahadur, late Central Prov. Commn.—Joined the service, 29th July, 1844; served in various subord. posts till Dec., 1864, when he was apptd. extra asst. commr.; C.I.E., Jan., 1882; asst. commr., Sept., 1883; settlt. officer, Jan., 1885; also asst. commr., June, 1890; retd., Dec., 1898.

AUSTIN, ROBERT FREDERICK, Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Madras).—Educ. at King's Coll. Sch. and Univ. Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1893; arrived, 23rd Dec., 1894, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.

AUSTIN, WARE PLUMPTRE, late Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch., and Gloucester Sch.; apptd. after exam. of 1862; arrived, 28th Dec., 1864, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr., and sub-collr. and joint mag.; district and sessions judge, Oct., 1884, and again in 1888; collr. and mag., Nov., 1886; collr. of sea customs, and of the Madras district, protector of emigrants, supt. of stamps and stationery, and chairman of the harbour trust board, Nov., 1891; retd., May, 1897.

AYLING, WILLIAM BOCK, Indian C.S. (head asst., Madras).—Educ. at Weymouth Coll. and Magdalene Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1886; arrived, 30th Jan., 1889, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; head assist. Oct., 1895.

BADCOCK, FRANCIS WILLIAM, late Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Harrow Sch.; apptd. after exam. of 1867; arrived, 24th Nov., 1869, and served in Bengal as asst. mag., joint mag., and dep. collr., and settlt. officer; offg. inspr.-gen. of registrn., March, 1879; joint mag. and dep. collr., Oct., 1881; district and sessions judge, Jan., 1883; retd., March, 1895.

BADDELEY, WILLIAM LEWIS CLINTON, Lieut.-Col., R.E., Madras Public Works Dept. (suptg. engr.).—Joined the dept., 23rd July, 1874; asst. engr., Aug., 1875; exec. engr., May, 1883; dep. chief engr. and under sec., public works dept., Nov., 1886, to March, 1891; suptg. engr., Oct., 1896.

BADEN-POWELL, BADEN HENRY, C.I.E., late Bengal C.S. (Punjab).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch.; apptd. after exam. of 1860; arrived, 31st Oct., 1861; asst. commr., May, 1862; from July, 1866, to April, 1869, served as small cause court judge, Lahore, and subsequently in the forest dept.; addl. commr., Lahore and Rawalpindi, Oct., 1881; dep. commr., Dec., 1882; judge, chief court, Punjab, March, 1889; ret'd., May, 1889, but employed for a time under govt. of India, rev. and agricl. dept.; has publ. 'Punjab Products,' 'Punjab Manufactures,' 'Manual of the Land Revenue System and Land Tenures of British India,' and 'Manual of Jurisprudence for Forest Officers.'

BADGLEY, WILLIAM FRANCIS, Col., I.S.C., late Survey of India.—First commn., April, 1857; arrived, May, 1857; served during the Mutiny in Central India and Oudh, and afterwards with the Lushai expedn. in charge of a survey party (medals and clasp); apptd. to the topogr. survey, May, 1865; and was employed in Rewa and Bundelkhand, and in the Khasi, Garo, and Naga Hills; dep. supt., Dec., 1880; in charge of Gujarat survey, March, 1884; offg. asst. surveyor-gen. in charge of photo. and litho. offices, March, 1886; ret'd., June, 1889.

BADOCK, HENRY WALTER (senr. clerk, Acctnt.-Gen's. Dept., India Office).—Junr. clerk, 11th Feb., 1876; senr. clerk, June, 1893.

BADROODIN TYABJI, Barr.-at-Law.—Judge of High Court, Bombay, July, 1895.

BAGLEY, FREDERICK ROBERT, M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept. (suptg. engr.).—Trained at Thomason Coll.; apptd., 16th Sept., 1871, and was employed on road works in the Central Prov., and on famine relief works in Bengal; transf. to state railways, and employed in Burma on railway survey and construction; exec. engr., May, 1883; suptg. engr., Dec., 1896; has received the India medal and two Burma clasps.

BAGNELL, HENRY WILLIAM JAMES, Indian C.S. (junr. collr. Bombay).—Educ. at Eastbourne Coll., Keble Coll., Oxford, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1880; arrived, 1st Nov., 1882, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and forest settl. officer; junr. collr., Jan., 1898.

BAGSHAW, CECIL (conservator, Forest Dept., Hyderabad Administration).—Joined the service as temporary sub-assst. conservator, 26th Oct., 1869; asst. conservator, 14th Aug., 1871; dep. conservator, Oct., 1876; employed in the mil. dept. in the Kuram valley, Aug., 1879, and at the forest sch., Dehra Dun, Dec., 1879; conservator, Jan., 1889.

BAGSHAW, FRANK THEODORE, late Madras Police Dept.—Entered the service May, 1869, and served in rev. dept. till June, 1876; then in police dept. as asst. supt. and supt.; dep. insp. gen. July, 1895; commr. of police, Madras city, Nov., 1895; acting inspr.-gen., Dec., 1896-May, 1897; ret'd., July, 1899.

BAILEY, FREDERICK, Lieut.-Col., R.E., late Forest Dept., N.W. Prov.—Arrived as a lieut., 12th Nov., 1864; served under the mil. dept. till

July, 1867, when he was apptd. asst. engr.; exec. engr., June, 1871; from Aug., 1871, served in the forest dept. as dep. conservator and supt. of forest surveys; conservator of forests, and dir. of Indian forest sch., in addn. to his duties as supt. of forest surveys, Sept., 1878; ret'd., April, 1892; is lecturer on forestry at Edinburgh univ., and sec. to royal Scottish geographical society.

BAILEY, RICHARD JONES, India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at Thomason Coll.; apptd., 25th Oct., 1872, and posted to the mil. works branch in the Punjab; transf. to Burma, Jan., 1881; exec. engr., May, 1883.

BAILLIE, DUNCAN COLVIN, Indian C.S. (opium agent, Benares).—Educ. at Inverness Acad., Glasgow Acad., and Edinburgh Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1877; arrived, 10th Nov., 1879, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr., asst. commr., and asst. settl. officer; joint mag., Sept., 1889; provl. supt. of census operations, April, 1890, and prepared the report; dep. commr., April, 1894; mag. and collr., Feb., 1896; sec. to Board of Revenue, July, 1896; opium agent, Benares, Jan., 1899.

BAINBRIDGE, GEORGE, M.D. (surgn.-gen. Bombay Med. Dept.).—Joined the service, 1st April, 1867; arrived, 25th Oct., 1867, and served in Bombay as civil surgn. and in various acting appts.; civil surgn., Karachi, Dec., 1891; princ. med. officer, Sind dist., Oct., 1896; surgn.-gen., Bombay, and member of plague committee, Oct., 1897.

BAINES, JERVOISE ATHELSTONE, C.S.I., late Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at Rugby and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1868; arrived, 12th Nov., 1870, and served as asst. collr. and mag., asst. pol. agent, and cantonmt. and railway mag.; dep. supt. of census operations in Bombay presidency, and under sec. to govt., gen. dept. (census), April, 1880; 2nd asst. collr. and forest settl. officer, Dec., 1882; on special duty to supervise the work under the income tax act in the Bombay presidency, May, 1886; also acting as dir. of agric. from Dec., 1886; 1st asst. collr. and mag., Oct.-Nov., 1886; served under the govt. of India as census commr. for India from Aug., 1889; on depn. to England to complete census report, May, 1892; on depn. at India office, July, 1893, and prepared decennial report on moral and material progress of India, 1891-92; C.S.I., Jan., 1894; sec. to royal commn. on opium, June, 1894, to March, 1895; ret'd., Sept., 1895.

BAIRD, ANDREW WILSON, C.S.I., F.R.S., Col., R.E., late Mint Dept., Calcutta.—Lieut., royal engrs., Dec., 1861; arrived, March, 1863, and served in the Bombay public works dept. as asst. engr., and was employed on the harbour defences and reclamations; exec. engr., Oct., 1867; served in the Abyssinian expedn. as asst. field engr., 1868 (medal); from Dec., 1868, served in the trigonol. survey as asst. surveyor, and was employed in Kathiawar and Gujarat; asst. supt., Oct., 1871; in July, 1877, was apptd. to the general superintendence and control of tidal observations upon the Indian coasts; on depn. as commr., Venice geographical congress and exhibn., Aug., 1881; dep. supt., July, 1882; mint master, Calcutta, Aug., 1889; C.S.I., June, 1897; ret'd., April, 1899; author of various papers and reports on tidal observations, and other works; and joint author of the annual tide tables for Indian ports.

BAKER, CHARLES MAURICE, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Bombay).—Educ. at Tonbridge Gr. Sch. and Trin. Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1894; arrived, 9th Dec., 1895, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.

BAKER, EDWARD NORMAN, Indian C.S. (collr. of customs, Bengal).—Educ. at Christ's Coll., Finchley; apptd. after exam. of 1876; arrived, 15th Nov., 1878, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; under sec., govt. of Bengal, finl. dept., May, 1885; acted as under sec., govt. of India, fin. and commerce dept., in 1885; dep. commr., June, 1890; dep. sec. to govt. of India, fin. and commerce dept., Sept., 1892; mag. and collr., Aug., 1895; collr. of customs, April, 1897.

BAKER, FRANCIS, late Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Berkhamstead Gr. Sch. and Cheltenham Coll.; apptd. after exam. of 1867; arrived, 13th Nov., 1869, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., and first asst. sec. to govt.; private sec. to lieut.-govr., N.W. Prov., April, 1874; junr. sec., board of rev., May, 1878; under sec. to govt., May, 1881; supt. of Dehra Dun, March, 1884; joint mag., Aug., 1886; dep. commr., March, 1888; mag. and collr., March, 1889; offg. commr., Allahabad, April, 1895; ret'd., Dec., 1896.

BAKER, HARRY VICTOR SAMPSON, India Public Works Dept. (asst. to chief engr., Punjab).—Trained at Cooper's Hill Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1875, and employed on irrign. works in the Punjab; exec. engr., May, 1883; under sec. to govt., Punjab, public works dept., 1892-94; personal asst. to chief engr., Punjab, May, 1898.

BAKER, MONTAGU BERNARD, late Bombay C.S.—Educ. at Winchester, and Pembroke Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1863; arrived, 29th Nov., 1864, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and in Sind as acting jud. commr. and judge of the sadar court; judge and sessions judge, May, 1882; ret'd., June, 1891.

BAKER, OSWALD, Surgn.-Lieut.-Col., late Med. Dept., Burma.—Joined the Bengal med. service, 1873; was employed under the mil. dept. up to May, 1874, and served subsequently in Burma as civil surgn. and supt. of jail; med. officer to the mission to Siam under Major C. W. Street, Feb., 1879; offg. inspr.-gen. of jails for Burma, May, 1887; joint med. officer, Simla, Sept., 1889; junr. civil surgeon, Rangoon, Nov., 1890; ret'd., July, 1898.

BAKER, WILLIAM THOMAS WEBB, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Bombay).—Educ. at Rugby and Exeter Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1896; arrived, 11th Dec., 1897, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.

BAKEWELL, JAMES HERBERT, LL.B., B.F. (dep. registrar, High Court, Madras).—Apptd. dep. registrar High Court, original side, Madras, May, 1896.

BALFOUR, CHARLES FREDERICK, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Clifton Sch. and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived, 8th Dec., 1896, and served in the N.W.P. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.

BALTHASAR, GUSTAVE, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Assam).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch. and Clare Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1891; arrived, 29th Nov., 1893, and served in Assam as asst. commr.

BAMBER, HENRY WILLIAM JOHN, late Police Dept., Bengal.—From 1st Oct., 1857, to May, 1862, served as asst. supt., and supt. of salt chowkies; from May, 1862, served in the Bengal police dept., and was promoted to district supt., 1st grade, Aug., 1885; ret'd., July, 1891.

BANDOPADHYA, KRISHNA CHANDRA, Rai Bahadur, A.M.I.C.E., B.A., Indian Public Works Dept. (inspr. of works).—Trained at the Thomason Civil Engrg. Coll.; apptd. to the public works dept., Sept., 1876; asst. engr., June, 1877; exec. engr., Jan., 1891; inspr. of works, western circle.

BANERJEE, DHARANIDHAR, Rai Bahadur, India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at Calcutta Civil Engrg. Coll.; apptd. to the dept., Oct., 1874, and posted to N.W. Prov. and Oudh; asst. engr., April, 1876; exec. engr., May, 1891.

BANERJEE, DURGAGATI, C.I.E., Rai Bahadur, Bengal Administration (collr. of stamp rev., Calcutta).—Joined the service, Aug., 1865, and held various subord. posts till April, 1889, when he was apptd. collr. of stamp rev., Calcutta, supt. of excise rev., &c.; C.I.E., Jan., 1895; member of provl. legisl. council, March, 1895.

BANERJEE, GOOROO DASS, M.A., D.L., (judge, Calcutta High Court).—Educ. at the Presidency Coll., Calcutta, and Calcutta Univ.; asst. lecturer on mathematics at that coll., 1865; law lecturer, Berhampur coll., 1866-72; Tagore law prof., 1878; judge, high court, Calcutta, Jan., 1889; member of Bengal legisl. council, 1888; vice-chancellor of Calcutta univ., 1890-92; author of 'The Hindu Law of Marriage and Stridhan.'

BANERJI, ALBION RAJ, KUMAR, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Madras).—Educ. at Calcutta Univ. and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1894; arrived 10th Dec., 1895, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.

BANKS, CHARLES, M.B. (supt. of emigration, Calcutta).—Entered the service 7th June, 1892; civil med. officer, Noakhali, Nov., 1893; protector of emigrants and supt. of emigration, Calcutta, May, 1897.

BANKS, SAMUEL HENRY O'BRIEN, F.R.C.S., Surgn.-Col., Bombay Med. Dept. (principal med. officer, Nagpur).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1866, and arrived, 17th April, 1867; served in the Abyssinian campaign, and afterwards on civil, mil., and jail duty; presidency surgn., Nov., 1888; principal med. officer, Nagpur, Oct., 1895.

BANNERMAN, PATRICK WILSON, Col., I.S.C., late Pol. Dept., Govt. of India.—Ensign, Bombay army, June, 1852; asst. to the supt., Nimach, July, 1859; asst. to govr.-gen.'s agent, Central India, 1860; pol. asst., Bhopawar, Aug., 1865; also Bhil agent and commandant, Malwa Bhil corps; pol. agent, Rewa, March, 1871; pol. agent, Bhopal, 1880; rest., E. states, Rajputana, Oct., 1881; rest., Gwalior, March, 1882; acted as agent to govr.-gen. for Central India in 1883-84, and again in 1887; reverted to mil. dept., 1888.

BANNERMAN, WILLIAM BURNEY, M.D., Major, Indian Med. Service (in charge plague laboratory, Bombay).—Joined the service 29th Sept., 1883; on mil. duty till 1891; inspr. of vaccn. and dep. sany. commr., Madras, Dec., 1893; in charge plague laboratory, Bombay, April, 1899.

BANTON, WILLIAM HERBERT PHILPE

(clerk, Registry and Record Dept., India Office).—Writer, India office, 5th Aug., 1875; clerk specially attached, 26th March, 1893.

BARADA CHARAN MITRA, M.A., Statutory C.S. (joint mag. and dep. collr., Bengal).—Apptd. under native civil service rules asst. mag. and collr., 30th March, 1886; confd., 23rd March, 1888; asst. settl. officer, Orissa, March, 1891; joint mag. and dep. collr., April, 1894.

BARBOUR, SIR DAVID MILLER, K.C.S.I., K.C.M.G., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Queen's Coll., Belfast; apptd. after exam. of 1862; arrived, 20th Dec., 1863, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; under sec., govt. of India, finl. dept., Aug., 1873; acctnt.-gen., Punjab, and dep. commr., paper currency, Lahore circle, June, 1877; offg. sec., govt. of Bengal, finl. dept., April, 1878; acctnt.-gen., Madras, and commr. of paper currency, Jan., 1880; acctnt.-gen., Bengal, Sept., 1881; offg. sec., govt. of Bengal, rev. and gen. depts., Jan., 1882; Bengal legisl. council, Jan. to Oct., 1882; sec., govt. of India, dept. of fin. and commerce, July, 1882; C.S.I., Feb., 1887; on special duty in England as member of the royal commn. on currency in 1886; finl. member of council of govr.-gen., Nov., 1888, to Nov., 1893; K.C.S.I., Jan., 1889; member of royal commn. on finl. relations between Great Britain and Ireland, May, 1894; apptd. a commr. to enquire into the finl. condition of the West Indies, 1896; member of Indian currency committee, 1898; K.C.M.G., June, 1899; author of 'The Theory of Bimetallism.'

BARCLAY, ERNEST (govt. solicitor, Madras).—Served from 18th Sept., 1861, as French interpreter to the supreme court, Madras, acting dep. clerk of the crown and acting asst. registrar; apptd. govt. solicitor, Feb., 1876.

BARCLAY, PRINGLE DAVID, late India Public Works Dept.—Joined the dept., March, 1875, as asst. traffic supt., Rajputana state railway; traffic supt., Punjab N. railway, March, 1881; E. Bengal railway, June, 1884; retd., 1896.

BARDSWELL, HUGH ROSSER, Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Madras).—Educ. at King's Coll. Sch., London, and Lincoln Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1897; arrived, 15th Dec., 1898, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.

BARKER, FREDERICK CHARLES, Lieut.-Col., I.M.S. (presy. surgn., Bombay).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1860; mil. duty to 15th Dec., 1871; civil surgn., Oct., 1874; presy. surgn., marine surgn., and in med. charge Elphinstone Coll., Oct., 1895.

BARKER, PHILIP LONGUEVILLE, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at the Charterhouse and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1897; arrived, 29th Nov., 1898, and served in the Punjab as assist. commr.

BARKLEY, DAVID GRAHAM, M.A., B.L., late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at Queen's Coll., Belfast; apptd. after exam. of 1857; arrived, 29th Dec., 1857, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Punjab as asst. mag. and collr., asst. commr., personal asst. to commr., Ambala, and asst. sessions judge; dep. commr., Oct., 1874; addl. commr., Delhi, May, 1876, and subsequently at Jalandhar and Lahore; offd. as judge, chief court, Punjab, 1878 and 1880; civil and sessions judge, Nov., 1880; commr. and supt., Multan, Sept., 1881; addl. judge, chief court, Jan., 1882; addl. member of govr.-gen.'s council, 1883 and 1884; judge of the chief court, Punjab, Feb.,

1886; retd., Jan., 1887; has publ. 'Directions to Rev. and Settlt. Officers, Non-Regulation Law.'

BARLOW, SIR RICHARD WELLESLEY, Bart., late Madras C.S.—Apptd. from Haileybury, 1855, and served in the rev. and jud. depts., Madras; from 1871 to 1878 served as collr. and mag. of Chengalpat, and subsequently as commr., Nilgiris, 1878-80, and 1881; major commandant Nilgiri volunteer rifles, 1878-79; acted as member of the board of rev. in 1880, and again in 1881 and 1886; collr. and mag., and addl. sessions judge, Nilgiris, Feb., 1882; collr. of sea customs, &c., Madras, Jan., 1883; acting rest., Travancore and Cochin, Feb. to Aug., 1884; member of the legisl. council, Madras, Sept., 1885; chairman of the harbour trust board, May, 1886; supt. of stamps and stationery, April, 1887, in addn.; retd., Nov., 1889.

BARNES, HERBERT CHARLES, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Assam).—Educ. at Westminster Sch. and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1893; arrived, 13th Dec., 1894, and served in Assam as asst. commr., and personal asst. to chief commr.

BARNES, HUGH SHAKESPEAR, C.S.I., Indian C.S., Foreign Dept., Govt. of India (rest. in Beluchistan).—Educ. at Malvern Coll.; apptd. after exam. of 1872; arrived, 30th Oct., 1874, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr.; transfed. to finl. dept. of govt. of India, Dec., 1876, and apptd. asst. acctnt.-gen. in the N.W. Prov.; private sec. to the finl. member of the govr.-gen.'s council, Dec., 1876; acted as under sec. in the home dept. in 1878; on special duty under the foreign dept., Nov., 1879; asst. to rest., Kandahar, Nov., 1880; served in the Afghan campaign, 1881, as pol. officer with Gen. Wilkinson's brigade; asst. to the govr.-gen.'s agent in Beluchistan, 1881-83; pol. agent in Quetta and Pishin, June, 1883; in charge of arrangements, supplies, &c., for passage of Afghan boundary commn. across Baluch desert, Aug., 1884; under sec. to govt. of India, foreign dept., May, 1890; rev. commr., Beluchistan, Dec., 1890; offd. as govr.-gen.'s agent and chief commr. in 1891 and 1892; rest., Kashmir, March, 1895; offd. as sec. to govt. of India, Foreign Dept., March, 1896, and again April, 1899; rest. and agent for govr.-genl. in Beluchistan, June, 1896; C.S.I., June, 1897.

BARNIVILLE, JOHN JOSEPH, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at the Univ. of Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived 3rd Dec., 1896, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

BARR, DAVID WILLIAM KEITH, C.S.I., Lieut.-Col., I.S.C., Foreign Dept., Govt. of India (agent in Central India).—First commn., Sept., 1864; served in Abyssinian expedn. (medal); boundary commr., Rewa Kantha, and Malwa Bhil agency, 1869; pol. asst. and boundary officer, Bhopal, July, 1869; asst. to govr.-gen.'s agent, Central India, 1870; offg. pol. agent, Jodhpur, 1878-79; pol. agent, Bhagelkhand, July, 1881; also supt., Rewa state; rest. at Gwalior, May, 1887; rest. in Kashmir, Nov., 1893; govr.-gen.'s agent in Central India, March, 1895; C.S.I., Jan., 1896.

BARRATT, CHARLES HENRY, India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at Cooper's Hill Coll.; entered the service as asst. engr., Sept., 1878; exec. engr., Oct., 1890.

BARRETT, EDWIN CLIFFORD, late Survey of India Dept.—Apptd. proby. asst. surveyor, Indus-

Sind rev. survey, 18th Nov., 1862; asst. supt., Dec., 1868; accompanied Lushai expdn., 1871-72 (medal and clasp); dep. supt., Oct., 1884; supt. (new scale), Aug., 1892; ret'd., April, 1898.

BARRON, ALBERT HENRY, late India Public Works Dept.—Joined the service, 27th May, 1868, as asst. engr., and was employed on irrig. work in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh; exec. engr., Sept., 1875; ret'd., Mar., 1898.

BARRON, CLAUD ALEXANDER, Indian C.S. (under sec. to govt., rev. dept., Punjab).—Educ. at the Aberdeen Gr. Sch., Aberdeen Univ., and Clare Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1890; arrived, 12th Nov., 1892, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; under sec. to govt., rev. dept., May, 1896.

BARROW, FRANK, Lieut.-Col., I.S.C., late Oudh Commn.—Educ. at Bruton Gr. Sch., Victoria Coll., Jersey, and Cheltenham Coll.; first commn., 6th Nov., 1863; from Aug., 1869, served as asst. commr. in Oudh, offg. junr. sec. to the chief commr., and offg. cantonmt. mag.; dep. commr., June, 1889; ret'd., May, 1896.

BARROW, FRANK HUNTER, late Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Bruton and Felsted Gr. Schs., and Cheltenham Coll. # apptd. after exam. of 1867; arrived, 17th Jan., 1870, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., Nov., 1881; mag. and collr., March, 1887; ret'd., Aug., 1894.

BARROW, OSCAR THEODORE, Indian C.S. (acct.-gen., Bombay).—Educ. at Gloucester Coll. Sch.; apptd. after exam. of 1875; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1877, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transfid. to finl. dept., govt. of India, Dec., 1882; asst. acctnt.-gen., Bombay, March, 1887; dep. acctnt.-gen., Bombay, April, 1887; offg. acctnt.-gen. and dep. commr., paper currency, N.W. Prov., May, 1888; acctnt.-gen., Bengal, April, 1891; acctnt.-gen., Bombay, March, 1894.

BARROW, REGINALD POCOCK, Indian C.S. (1st asst., Bombay).—Educ. at Oxford Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1883; arrived, 13th Nov., 1885, and served in Sind as asst. collr. and mag., 2nd asst., and forest settlt. officer; 1st asst., July, 1896.

BARROW, WILLIAM FRANCIS, India Public Works dept. (examiner of accounts, N.W.P.)—Joined the service as accountant, May, 1874; served in the office of examiner of public works accounts, asst. examiner, July, 1877; dept. examiner, July, 1880; examiner, May, 1886; examiner of public works accounts, N.W. Prov. and Oudh, Mar., 1898.

BARRY, JOHN PATRICK, Major, Indian Med. Service, Bombay (supt., Colaba Asylum).—Joined the service, April, 1882; on mil. duty to 1892; supt., Matheran, Oct., 1892; civil surgn., Kaira, June, 1897; supt., Colaba Lunatic Asylum, April, 1898.

BARRY, THOMAS DAVID COLLIS, Major, Indian Med. Service, Bombay (prof'r., Grant med. coll.).—Joined the dept., 27th May, 1889, as acting chemical analyser, prof'r. of chemistry and med. jurisprudence, Grant med. coll., and prof'r. of chemistry, Elphinstone coll.; confd. in these posts, April, 1892.

BARRY, WILLIAM, M.R.C.S., late Bengal C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at the High Sch. and Univ. of Glasgow, and Univ. of Edinburgh; apptd. after exam. of 1861; arrived, 14th Feb., 1863, and served in the N.W. Prov. as

asst. mag. and collr.; employed on famine duty in Bengal, 1873-75; joint mag. and dep. collr., Banda, Aug., 1877; from May, 1881, served in the jud. dept. and was apptd. district sessions judge, Nov., 1882; ret'd., March, 1890.

BARSTOW, HENRY CLEMENTS, late Bengal C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1860; arrived, 31st Oct., 1861, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., and joint mag. and dep. collr.; served under the govt. of India, foreign dept., Jan., 1874, to Oct., 1875, as offg. pol. agent, Bhopal; offg. supt., Dehra Dun, Feb., 1876; mag. and collr., Cawnpore, April, 1880; ret'd., Nov., 1886.

BARTER, JOSEPH FRANCIS, Brigade-Surgn., late Madras Med. Dept.—Joined the service, July, 1859; from April, 1862, served in the Central Prov. as civil surgn. and supt. of jail, and acted for various periods as inspr.-gen. of jails; sanitary commr., supt. of vaccn., and inspr.-gen. of dispensaries, Dec., 1878; civil surgn. and supt. of lunatic asylum, Nagpur, July, 1883; ret'd., April, 1890.

BARTHOLOMEUSZ, MATHEW LORENZ, M.B., C.M., Lieut.-Col., Indian Med. Service, (civil surgn., Ahmadabad).—Joined the service, 30th Sept., 1873, and served on mil. duty, and as med. officer at Zanzibar; civil surgn., Sukkur, April, 1884; transfid. to Nasik, June, 1888; civil surgn., supt. of lunatic asylum, &c., Ahmadabad, Nov., 1889.

BARTHOLOMEW, ROBERT, Lieut.-Col., Bengal Cavalry, late Punjab Commn.—Arrived 2nd April, 1862; from 1st Aug., 1866, served in the Punjab commn. as asst. commr.; jud. asst., Sept., 1881; district judge, 1st grade, Delhi, offg. as divl. judge, Nov., 1884; dep. commr., July, 1886; ret'd., April, 1894.

BARTLETT, THOMAS WALTER, M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Joined the service, 31st Jan., 1871, as asst. engr., and was employed on Rajputana state railway; exec. engr., Jan., 1880; supt. of works, Bolan railway, April, 1885; services lent to Bengal-Nagpur railway co., Jan., 1888; transfid. to Madras, Nov., 1889, and employed on Villupuram-Guntakal and E. Bengal state railways.

BARTON, FRANCIS JOHN HERBERT, Captain, Indian Staff Corps, Pol. Dept., Punjab.—Joined the service, 23rd April, 1881; in mil. employ till July, 1892; commdtd. Port Blair, July, 1892; European asst. to pol. offr., Khyber, April, 1897; commdtd., Khyber rifles, May, 1897.

BARTON, WILLIAM PELL, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at Bedford Modern Sch. and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1893; arrived, 17th Dec., 1894, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; under sec. to govt., Oct., 1898.

BATCHELOR, ERNEST, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Central Provs.).—Educ. at Harrow and Caius Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1896; arrived, 12th Dec., 1897, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr.

BATCHELOR, STANLEY LOCKHART, B.A., Indian C.S. (2nd asst., Bombay).—Educ. at St. Edmund's Coll., Ware, and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1887; arrived, 1st Dec., 1889, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; 2nd asst., May, 1896; under sec. to govt., pol. dept., Sept., 1896.

BATE, THOMAS ELWOOD LINDSAY, Lieut.-Col.,

Indian Med. Service (inspr.-gen. of prisons, Punjab).—Joined the service, 31st March, 1875; arrived, 14th Nov., 1875; served in mil. dept. to Nov., 1880, when he was apptd. civil surgn., Gujrat; inspr.-gen. of prisons, Punjab, April, 1891.

BATHURST, JAMES ALEXANDER, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Cent. Provs.).—Educ. at Bradford and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1897; arrived 22nd Nov., 1898, and served in the Cent. Provs. as assist. commr.

BATTEN, HALLET GEORGE (dep. commr., Burma).—Joined the service, 17th Oct., 1869, and on 13th Feb., 1871, was apptd. extra asst. commr., Burma; asst. commr., Nov., 1877, and dep. commr., May, 1888; served also as cantonmt. mag. and judge of small cause court in 1886-87.

BATTEN, JOHN KAYE, Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Madras).—Educ. at Haileybury Coll., and at Ariel Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1884; arrived, 29th Nov., 1887, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; special asst., Malabar, Nov., 1893; head asst., Jan., 1895; dep. commr., April, 1899.

BATTY, HERBERT, M.A., B.L., Indian C.S. (remembrancer of legal affairs, Bombay).—Educ. at Christ's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1868; arrived, 31st Oct., 1870, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., profr. of logic and moral philosophy in the Deccan coll., Poona (1872-75), and asst. judge and sessions judge; under sec. to govt. of Bombay, pol., &c., depts., and sec. to the legisl. council, Nov., 1885; acting chief presidency mag., Bombay, April, 1888; remembrancer of legal affairs, June, 1892; member of legisl. council, June, 1893, and again, July, 1895; acting chief sec. to govt., pol., &c., depts., July, 1896; acting judl. commr. in Sind, April, 1899.

BAXTER, BERNARD, India Public Works Dept. (engr.-in-chief).—Trained at Thomason Coll.; apptd., 1st Nov., 1871, and employed on state railways in Sind and Baluchistan; transfld. to Bengal, April, 1881; exec. engr., May, 1883; employed in Burma, April, 1888; and in Madras, on E. Coast railway, from Nov., 1889; engineer-in-chief, July, 1897.

BAYLAY, CHARLES ALLEN, Col., I.S.C., late Foreign Dept., Govt. of India.—Lieut., Bengal artillery, Dec., 1857; asst. commr., Berar, Jan., 1866, and subsequently in Ludhiana; pol. asst., Rajputana, 1868-70; asst. gen. supt. for thagi and dakaiti, Dec., 1870; pol. agent, Haraoti and Tonk, 1876; pol. agent, Kotah, Dec., 1879; acted as rest., W. states of Rajputana, in 1884; ret'd., May, 1890.

BAYLEY, CHARLES STUART, B.L., Indian C.S. (gen. supt., thagi and dakaiti dept.).—Educ. at Harrow and Heidelberg; apptd. after exam. of 1875; arrived, 31st Dec., 1877, and served as asst. mag. and collr. in Bengal, offg. asst. sec. to chief commr. of Assam, July, 1880, and cffg. under sec. to govt. of Bengal; member of exec. committee, Calcutta international exhibn. of 1883-84; offg. registrar, high court, March, 1884; under sec., govt. of India, rev. and agricl. dept., April, 1885; asst. commr., Ajmir, Sept., 1886; pol. agent, Bikanir, June, 1888; joint mag. and dep. collr., Jan., 1889; pol. agent, Bikanir, Sept., 1891; addl. private sec. to viceroy, Dec., 1894, rest. at Jaipore, April, 1897; gen. supt., thagi and dakaiti dept., April, 1893.

BAYLEY, SIR LYTTLETON HOLYOAKE, Kt., B.L., late Jud. Dept., Bombay.—Called to the bar (Middle Temple), May, 1850; apptd. advocate-gen., Bombay, 17th March, 1866; judge of the high court, Bombay, June, 1869; acted as chief justice for various periods; ret'd., June, 1895; Kt., Jan., 1896.

BAYLEY, SIR STEUART COLVIN, K.C.S.I., C.I.E., late Bengal C.S. (member of the Council of India).—Apptd. from Haileybury; arrived, 4th March, 1856, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., junr. sec. to govt., and mag. and collr.; commr., Dacca divn., July, 1873, and Patna divn., Sept., 1873; sec., govt. of Bengal, May, 1877; addl. sec., govt. of India, finl. dept., Aug., 1877; personal asst. to the viceroy, for famine affairs, Sept., 1877; addl. sec., govt. of India, public works dept., famine branch, in addn., Dec., 1877; sec., govt. of India, home dept., June, 1878; offg. chief commr., Assam, June, 1878; offg. lieut.-govr. of Bengal, July-Nov., 1879; chief commr. of Assam, June, 1880; rest., Haidarabad, March, 1881; member of govr.-gen.'s council, May, 1882; lieut.-govr. of Bengal, April, 1887; ret'd., Dec., 1890; apptd. sec., pol. and secret dept., India office, Dec., 1890; member of council of India, Sept., 1895.

BAYLY, WILLIAM GOODENOUGH, B.A., late India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. to the accts. branch as asst. controller, 28th July, 1868; dep. controller, Nov., 1871; asst. examr., April, 1872; dep. examr., July, 1877; examr., Jan., 1882; examr., public works accts., Burma, Feb., 1889; ret'd., Aug., 1893.

BAYNE, CHARLES GERWIEN, Indian C.S. (rev. sec., Burma).—Educ. at Uppingham, and Univ. Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1878; arrived, 27th Dec., 1880, and served in British Burma as asst. commr.; junr. sec. to chief commr., April, 1886; under sec., April, 1887; dep. commr., Nov., 1888; junr. sec. to chief commr., Dec., 1890; sec., March, 1891; offg. chief sec., March-July, 1892; rev. sec., March, 1893.

BEACHCROFT, CHARLES PORTER, Indian C.S. (asst. mag., and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Rugby, and Clare Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1890; arrived, 29th Nov., 1892, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

BEACHCROFT, FRANCIS PORTEN, B.A., B.L., late Bengal C.S. (Punjab).—Educ. at Rugby, and Exeter Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1863; arrived, 12th Nov., 1864, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; jud. asst., April, 1878; dep. commr., Dec., 1883; divl. judge, Nov., 1884; offd. as judge of chief court, Punjab, in 1890 and 1891; ret'd., Nov., 1891.

BEADON, CECIL, Colonel, Madras Cavalry, late Punjab Commn.—Arrived, 24th March, 1858; from May, 1862, served in the Punjab commn. as asst. commr., cantonmt. mag., and jud. asst.; dep. commr., March, 1877; offd. as commr. and supt. at various periods; reverted to mil. dept., Oct., 1893.

BEADON, GUY CECIL, Capt. Indian Staff Corps (dist. judge, Punjab).—Arrived, 11th Oct., 1884; in mil. employ till Dec., 1887; served in Punjab commn. as asst. commr.; dist. judge, June, 1894.

BEAMAN, FRANK CLEMENT OFFLEY, Indian C.S. (judge and sessions judge, Bombay).—Educ. at Bedford Gr. Sch., and Queen's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1877; arrived,

22nd Dec., 1879, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; from April, 1883, served in the jud. dept.; special settlt. officer, Baroda, 1886 and 1887; asst. judge, April, 1890; jud. asst. to the pol. agent in Kathiawar, May, 1891; judge and sess. judge, Jan., 1896.

BEAMES, HENRY BLUNT, late Stamps and Stationery Dept., Bengal.—Entered the service June, 1868, and served as dep. mag. and dep. collr. and inspr. of registrn. offices; apptd. supt. of stamps and stationery, Oct., 1896; retd., Dec., 1898.

BEAMES, JOHN, late Bengal C.S.—Apptd. from Haileybury; arrived, 16th March, 1858; from March, 1859, served in the Punjab as asst. commr., and from Dec., 1861, in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and joint mag.; mag. and collr., July, 1867; commr., Bardwan, Nov., 1881; offg. member of board of rev., 1887 and 1889; commr., Bhagalpur, Aug., 1887; prest. of the police revision committee, Aug., 1890; commr., Presidency divn., Nov., 1891; retd., March, 1893; author of 'Outlines of Indian Philology,' 1867, 'Comparative Grammar of the Modern Aryan Languages of India,' 1872-79, and 'Grammar of the Bengali Language,' 1891; edited Sir H. M. Elliot's 'Races of the N.W. Provinces of India,' 1869.

BEAN, MAURICE, Capt. R.I.M., late Port Officer, Bombay.—Joined the service as asst. port officer, Bombay; offg. port officer, Madras, April, 1891; port officer, Bombay, Oct., 1891; retd., Oct., 1896.

BEATSON-BELL, NICHOLAS DODD, B.A., Indian C.S. (joint mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Clifton Bank, St. Andrews, Edinburgh Acad., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1886; arrived, 16th Dec., 1889, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., July, 1895; joint mag. and collr., Nov., 1896.

BEATTY, JOSEPH, Col., R.E., late Public Works Dept., Madras.—First commn., Dec., 1852; arrived, 6th Jan., 1855, and served in the Madras public works dept. as asst. engr. and exec. engr.; supervisor of the canals, Godavari, May, 1874; suptg. engr., Aug., 1877; retd., May, 1889.

BEATTY, THOMAS BERKELEY, B.A., M.D., Surgn.-Gen., late Bombay Med. Service.—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Dublin; joined the Bombay med. service, May, 1851; civil surgn., Karachi, July, 1853; supt. of vaccn., April, 1856; supt., Matheran, Dec., 1867; dep. surgn.-gen., Poona, Oct., 1876; principal med. officer, Malta expedy. force, 1878; surgn.-gen. with the govt. of Bombay, April, 1880; retd., March, 1885; author of various contributions on med. subjects in the transactions of the med. and physical society, Bombay.

BEAUMONT, THOMAS, M.D., F.R.C.S.I., Dep. Surgn.-Gen., late Madras Med. Dept.—Arrived, 13th Sept., 1857; served under the mil. dept. till Feb., 1864, when he was apptd. residency surgn., Indore; residency surgn., Hyderabad, June, 1881; also supt. of vaccn. and supt. of med. sch.; retd., Oct., 1885.

BEAUMONT, WILLIAM, late Rev. Survey Dept., Madras.—Joined the dept., 27th May, 1858, and served as asst. supt. and dep. supt.; member of reorganisation committee, Madras, March, 1871, and of board of examrs., Nov., 1882; retd., Nov., 1887.

BEAVAN, REGINALD, Col., I.S.C., late Survey of India Dept.—Ensign, 4th May, 1860; arrived, 11th June, 1860; in July, 1868, joined the topogr. survey dept. as asst. surveyor; served with various survey parties, and in July, 1880, organised and took charge of the Baluchistan topogr. survey; dep. supt., Nov., 1884; retd., June, 1892; served in S. Afghanistan, 1878 (medal); accompanied the Marri field force and the Bozdar field force in 1880 and 1881.

BECHER, ALFRED READ, India Public Works Dept. (acctnt.-gen., and dep. sec. to govt. of India).—Joined the dept., 23rd July, 1868; served in the Punjab as asst. engr. on the Sirhind canal; from April, 1873, served in the accts. branch, Punjab, as asst. controller, and dep. controller; transf. to Bombay, Nov., 1875; served subsequently in Hyderabad, Oudh, Rajputana, and Mysore; examr. of accts., Calcutta, June, 1886; dep. acctnt.-gen. and under sec. to govt. of India, July, 1893; acctnt.-gen., and dep. sec. to govt. of India, Dec., 1897.

BECKETT, HENRY BARRON, late Punjab Commn.—Served from 10th May, 1860, as asst. commr. and jud. asst.; dep. commr., Peshawar, Aug., 1884; district judge, Oct., 1885; dep. commr., Aug., 1886; retd., 1897.

BEDFORD, CHARLES AUGUSTUS S. (dep. commr. Bengal).—Joined the service, 25th July, 1864, as asst. commr.; civil asst. pol. officer, Lushai expedy., Feb., 1889; dep. commr., April, 1891.

BEDFORD, JAMES PERCH, Indian C.S. (head asst., Madras).—Educ. at Clifton Coll. and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1887; arrived, 14th Dec., 1889, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; asst. acctnt.-gen., Nov., 1895; dep. acctnt.-gen., Nov., 1897; head asst., Nov., 1898.

BEESTON, ROBERT CHAPMAN, late India Public Works Dept.—Joined the service, 30th Aug., 1873, and was employed on state railways in Central India and Burma; exec. engr., Feb., 1881; on special duty in Upper Burma in 1885-86; employed on Kashmir railway survey, Feb., 1891; and on N.W. line, Jan., 1893; retd., June, 1899.

BEGBIE, ALFRED GLYNN, Lieut.-Col., R.E., late India Public Works Dept.—First commn., July, 1861; arrived, Nov., 1870; joined the dept., Feb., 1871, as asst. engr. in the railway dept., Central India; exec. engr., Oct., 1871; Agra-Gwalior railway survey, Nov., 1872; offg. dep. controller of accts., Bengal, Aug., 1874; controller of accts., Mysore, Aug., 1875; examr., guaranteed railway accts., Calcutta, 1878; Punjab N. railway accts., 1880; dep. acctnt.-gen., and under sec. to govt. of India, public works dept., Jan., 1886; acctnt.-gen. and dep. sec., Oct., 1889; retd., July, 1893.

BEHARI LAL GUPTA, B.L., Indian C.S. (district and sessions judge, Bengal).—Educ. at Colootola Branch Sch., Calcutta, Calcutta Univ., and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1869; arrived, 29th Sept., 1871, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; on famine relief duty, Sept., 1874; presidency mag. and coroner, Calcutta, June, 1881; district and sessions judge, Nov., 1888.

BEIGHTON, THOMAS DURANT, late Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at King Edward's Sch., Birmingham; apptd. after exam. of 1865; arrived, 4th Dec., 1867; and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and joint mag. and dep. collr.;

on special duty in connection with boundary disputes on the Bhutan frontier, Jan., 1878; district and sessions judge, May, 1885; acted as supt. and remembrancer of legal affairs in 1894; member of provl. legisl. council, March, 1895; ret'd., May, 1896; author of 'The Ethnography of the Bhutan Frontier,' and two pamphlets.

BELCHAMBERS, ROBERT (registrar, Calcutta high court).—Joined the service, 15th Sept., 1848; dep. registrar, Calcutta high court, Feb., 1851; registrar, Oct., 1862; is also keeper of records, taxing officer, acctnt.-gen., and sealer; author of 'Practice of the Civil Courts,' and other legal publs.

BELL, CHARLES ALFRED, Indian C.S. (joint mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Winchester, and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1839; arrived, 3rd Nov., 1891, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr. and asst. settl. officer; joint mag. and collr., June, 1898.

BELL, HAROLD FREDERIC ELLWOOD, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Central Provs.).—Educ. at Bradford Gr. Sch. and Pembroke Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1897; arrived, 6th Dec., 1898, and served in the Cent. Prov. as assist. commr.

BELL, HENRY JAMES, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Gordon's Coll., Aberdeen, Aberdeen Univ., and St. Peter's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1888; arrived, 26th Nov., 1890, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. Jan., 1896; asst. supt. and judge, small cause court, Mar., 1896; asst. commr., April, 1899.

BELL, HORACE, M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. proby. asst. engr., 7th July, 1862; served in the Central Prov. and Central India, and was promoted to exec. engr., 1st grade, March, 1871; subsequently served on the Punjab, Rajputana, and Sindh and Nimach state railways; suptg. engr., Jan., 1880; transfd. to Bengal, Nov., 1881; engr.-in-chief, Dacca and Maimansingh railway surveys, Dec., 1881; manager and engr.-in-chief, Tirhut state railway, Feb., 1884; acting dir., N.W. railway, July—Oct., 1887; engr.-in-chief, Great Western of India railway survey, Sept., 1888, and of the Moghal Sarai Howrah railway survey, Feb., 1889; consulting engr. to the govt. of India for railways, Aug., 1890; chief engr., Oct., 1890; offg. dir.-gen. of railways, and dep. sec., Jan. to April, 1893, when he reverted to his post of consulting engr.; ret'd., June, 1894; author of 'Laws of Wealth,' a work on pol. economy for native readers, and of 'Railway Policy in India.'

BELL, JAMES RICHARD, M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Joined the service, 8th Oct., 1868, and served as asst. engr., exec. engr., and supt. of works in various projects; engr.-in-chief, Haidarabad-Umarkot railway survey, Jan., 1890; in charge of Mari extension of the Sind-Sagar branch of the N.W. railway, Feb., 1890; suptg. engr., Oct., 1890; engr.-in-chief of the Mari-Attock extension railway and frontier railway surveys, Dec., 1891; engr.-in-chief, E. Coast railway, May, 1893; consulting engr. to the govt. of India for state railways, June, 1894; rank of chief engr., July, 1894; ret'd., Aug., 1896.

BELL, MATTHEW DENNY, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Madras).—Educ. at Bedford Gr.

Sch., and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1879; arrived, 25th Nov., 1881, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub-collr. and joint mag., Dec., 1894; district and sessions judge, Oct., 1897.

BELL, WILLIAM, M.A. (inspr. of schools, Punjab).—Joined the service, 16th Oct., 1855, as principal, central training coll., Lahore; profir. in the govt. coll. there, March, 1888; principal, April, 1892; inspr. of schools, Lahore Circle; author of a number of educ. works.

BELLASIS, EDWARD SKELTON, A.M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at Cooper's Hill Coll.; apptd., 1st Oct., 1875, and employed on irrign. works in the Punjab; exec. engr., May, 1883.

BELLINGHAM, HORACE COHEN (clerk, Finl. Dept., India Office).—Writer, India office, 18th May, 1875; clerk specially attached, Sept., 1892.

BENETT, WILLIAM CHARLES, late Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Marlborough Coll., and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1865; arrived, 4th Dec., 1867, and served in Oudh as asst. commr. and asst. settl. officer, Gonda, till April, 1876; dir. of agric. and commerce, N.W. Prov. and Oudh, April, 1881; sec., board of rev., May, 1884; offg. sec. to govt., rev. and jud. depts., 1886, confirmed 1888; offg. chief sec. in 1887, 1889, and 1891; offg. sec. to govt. of India, rev. and agric. depts., April—Oct., 1890; member of legisl. council, 1889-91; settl. commr., Oudh, July, 1894; ret'd., Oct., 1896; author of 'History of the Chief Clans of the Rae Bareli District,' contributions to the Oudh Gazetteer, and the Gonda settl. report.

BENGER, ALFRED (senr. clerk, India Audit Office).—Temporary clerk, 20th Nov., 1866; junr. asst., June, 1867; senr. examr., July, 1877; designation changed to senr. clerk, Oct., 1892.

BENNETT, WILLIAM EXALL TEMPEST, India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at Thomason Coll.; apptd. to the dept. as apprentice engr., Oct., 1878; asst. engr., Oct., 1879; exec. engr., Aug., 1893.

BENSLBY, EDWIN CLEMENT, Surgn.-Major, late Bengal Med. Service.—First commn., 1859; served from 22nd July, 1863, as civil asst. surgn. at Midnapur and Rajshahi; civil surgn., Rajshahi, Oct., 1875; ret'd., Nov., 1885.

BENSON, CHARLES, M.R.A.C. (dep. dr. Madras Rev. Dept.).—Joined the service, 30th Jan., 1874, as asst. supt., Saidapet farms; asst. dir. of agric., April, 1884; asst. commr. of land records and agric., April, 1887; asst. dir. of that dept., Nov., 1887; dep. dir., Jan., 1892.

BENSON, RALPH SILLERY, M.A., LL.B., B.L., Indian C.S. (judge, High Court, Madras).—Educ. at Rathmines Sch., and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1871; arrived, 29th Oct., 1873, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; acted as head asst. collr. and mag. of Cuddapah during the famine of 1876-78; specially apptd. officer for the settl. of lands in the Nilgiri districts; head asst., N. Arcot, June, 1882; sec. to the local self-govt. committee, Oct., 1882; forest settl. officer, May, 1883; acting under sec. to govt. in 1885 and 1887; acting sec. to the commrs. of land rev., 1887-88; sub-collr. and joint mag., Oct., 1887; district and sessions judge, Nov., 1889; collr. and mag., Feb., 1895; judge, High Court, March, 1896; author of the settl. registers and

report, and descriptive memoirs, of the Nilgiri district.

BENSON, THOMAS, B.A., late Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Tavistock Gr. Sch., Marlborough Coll., and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1866; arrived, 14th Nov., 1868, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., asst. settlt. officer, and manager of the Awa estate; asst. commr., Oudh, Oct., 1883; joint mag., Nov., 1883; district and sessions judge, April, 1887; ret'd., Jan., 1896.

BENTINCK, ARTHUR HAROLD WALTER, Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Harrow and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1897; arrived 6th Dec., 1898, and served in Bengal as assist. mag. and collr.

BENTON, ALEXANDER HAY, M.A., late Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educ. at the Univ. and King's Coll., Aberdeen; apptd. after exam. of 1861; arrived, 20th Feb., 1864, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Punjab as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. commr.; jud. asst., Nov., 1876; dep. commr., Jan., 1879; divl. judge, Nov., 1884; judge of chief court, Nov., 1889; ret'd., July, 1894.

BENTON, JOHN, India Public Works Dept. (supt. engr.).—Trained at Cooper's Hill Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1873, and employed on irrig. work in the Punjab; exec. engr., Oct., 1881; suptg. engr., Mandalay, Oct., 1897.

BERESFORD, JOHN STUART, C.I.E., M.E., M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept. (chief engr. and sec. to govt., Punjab).—Joined the service as asst. engr., N.W. Prov., 26th July, 1867; exec. engr., Sept., 1872; personal asst. to chief engr., irrig., May, 1877, and 1884-85; suptg. engr., Dec., 1889; joint sec. to govt., N.W. Prov. and Oudh, Aug., 1892; chief engr., Feb., 1895; sec. to chief commr., Central Prov., public works dept., June, 1895; chief engr. and sec. to govt., Punjab, Oct., 1896; C.I.E. Jan., 1900.

BERESFORD, LORD WILLIAM LESLIE DE LA POER, K.C.I.E., V.C., Col.—First commn., June, 1867; served in the Jowaki-Afridi expdn., 1877-78 (medal and clasp); also in the Zulu war of 1879 (V. C., medal, and clasp); and in the Afghan war, 1878-80 (medal and clasp); A.D.C. to viceroy of India, 1875-81; mil. sec. to viceroy, Dec., 1881 to Dec., 1893; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1894; served with the Burmese expdn. of 1886, as mil. sec. to govr.-gen. (clasp).

BERKELEY, JAMES CAVAN, C.I.E., Maj.-Gen., I.S.C., late Foreign Dept., Govt. of India.—First commn., Madras infantry, Oct., 1857; served from June, 1862, as asst. to the govr.-gen.'s agent in Central India; also as boundary settlt. officer, dep. opium agent in Malwa, asst. gen. supt. for thagi and dakaiti, and sessions judge and registrar for Mhow; pol. agent, Haraoti and Tonk, 1873; pol. agent, Baghelkhand, Jan., 1879; pol. agent, Jacobabad, 1882; offg. rest., Gwalior, March, 1883; on special duty, Kashmir, 1884; pol. agent, Bundelkhand, 1884; offg. rest., Nipal, 1885; rest. and govr.-gen.'s agent at Baroda, April, 1886; reverted to mil. dept., Nov., 1887.

BERNARD, SIR CHARLES EDWARD, K.C.S.I., late Bengal C.S., (sec., Rev. and Statistics Dept., India Office).—Apptd. to the Bengal civil service from Haileybury; arrived, 12th Feb., 1858, and

served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; undet. sec., govt. of India, finl. dept., Aug., 1861; from Oct., 1862, served in the Central Prov. as judge of the small cause court, Nagpur, settlt. officer, sec. to the chief commr. (July, 1864); commr. of Nagpur (Feb., 1869) and acting jud. commr.; from May, 1871, to Jan., 1875, served in Bengal as member of legisl. council, and acting sec. to govt., first in the jud., then in the rev., and lastly in the scarcity and relief dept.; from Nov., 1876, served as commr. of Nagpur divn.; sec. to Sir Richard Temple, depd. on a special mission to Bombay and Madras, Jan., 1877; jud. commr., Central Prov., Aug., 1877; addl. sec. to govt. of India in the dept. of rev., agric., and commerce, Aug., 1877, and in the finl. dept., Dec., 1877; sec. to govt. of India in the home dept., May, 1878, and in rev. and agricl. dept., July, 1881; from July, 1880, acted as chief commr. of British Burma; confd., April, 1882; ret'd., March, 1888, and was apptd. sec. in the rev. and statistics dept., India office.

BERNARD, JAMES HENRY, Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Clifton Coll., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1882; arrived, 30th Dec., 1884; asst. mag. and collr., Midnapur, Jan., 1885; personal asst. to chief commr., British Burma, June, 1886; returned to Bengal, Feb., 1887, as offg. under sec.; joint mag. and dep. collr., Dec., 1891; mag. and collr. Feb., 1896.

BERRILL, EDWARD, late Police Dept., N.W. Prov. and Oudh.—Served in the Mutiny in Barrow's volunteer cavalry; adjutant, mil. police, Central Prov., 14th Aug., 1858; inspr. of police, N.W. Prov., Feb., 1861, and asst. inspr.-gen., Dec., 1861; district supt., May, 1867; dep. inspr.-gen. of police, March, 1893; ret'd., April, 1897.

BERRINGTON, TREVOR DOUGLAS DAVIES, (supt. India Telegraph Dept.).—Trained at Cooper's Hill Coll.; apptd. asst. supt., Oct., 1878; and posted to the Burma divn.; supt., April, 1890.
