HUMPHREYS, ROBERT, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at the Royal Univ. of Ireland and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1893; arrived, 29th Dec., 1894, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

HUMPHRIES, EDGAR DE MONTFORT, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at the Grammar Sch., Lichfield, and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived, 9th Dec. 1896, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.

HUMPHRYS, ISAAC HUTCHINSON (senior clerk, Acctnt.-Gen.'s Dept., India Office).—Educ. at the Royal Sch., Raphoe, and Trinity Coll., Dublin (exhibr. and prizeman); clerk, 2nd class, 5th July, 1872; junr. clerk, Feb., 1876; senr. clerk, Sept., 1894.

HUNT, SAMUEL BRADSHAW, Surgn.-Col., late Indian Med. Service.—Asst. surgn., 31st March, 1865; arrived, 10th Dec., 1865, and served under the mil. dept., and also as civil surgn. in Madras; surgn., 4th district, and prof. of med. jurisprudence, med. coll., Feb., 1893; reverted to mil. dept., Aug., 1893, and apptd. princ. med. officer, Bangalore and Southern districts; retd., March, 1898.

HUNTER, JOHN MUIR, C.S.I., Lieut.-Col., I.S.C., Pol. Dept., Bombay (pol. agent, Kathiawar).—First commn., royal artillery, 12th April, 1864; arrived, 14th Dec. 1865; boundary settlt. officer, Baroda and Mahi Kantha states, Oct., 1869; from June, 1872, served as asst. pol. agent in Mahi Kantha, Kolhapur, and Kathiawar; from Feb., 1883, served as supt. of surveys under the rajasthanik court, Kathiawar, and was employed in settling claims in dispute between native states, from Dec., 1884; prest., rajasthanik court, May, 1891; asst. agent, Baroda, Oct., 1891; first asst. pol. agent, Mahi Kantha, Jan., 1892; pol. agent, Mahi Kantha, July, 1893, to Jan., 1894, and Nov., 1894, to March, 1895; prest., rajasthanik court, January, 1894; pol. agent, Kolhapur, April, 1896; pol. agent, Kathiawar, April, 1897; C.S.I., June, 1899.

HUNTER, SIR WILLIAM GUYER, K.C.M.G., M.D., F.R.C.P., F.R.C.S., Surgn.-Gen.—Educ. at King's Coll., London, Aberdeen Univ., &c.; joined the Bombay med. service, 1850; served in the Burmese war and Indian Mutiny; principal of the Grant med. coll., Bombay, 1868; surgn.-gen., Bombay, Feb., 1877; vice-chancellor of the univ. of Bombay, 1879; retd., March, 1880; served on the cholera commn. in Egypt, 1883; British delegate to the international sanitary conference at Rome, June, 1885; M.P. for Hackney (central divn.), 1885 and 1886-92.

HUNTER, SIR WILLIAM WILSON, K.C.S.I., C.I.E., M.A., LL.D., late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at the Univs. of Glasgow, Paris, and Bonn; apptd. after exam. of 1861; arrived, 10th Nov., 1862, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; supt. of labour transport, Kushtia, July, 1865; dir. and compiler of the statistical acct. of Bengal, July, 1869; compiled plan for a statistical survey of India, Jan., 1871; offg. under sec., govt. of India, home dept., Feb., 1871; dir.-gen. of statistics, govt. of India, Sept., 1871; on special duty to England for purpose of compiling statistical acct. of Bengal, and 'Imperial Gazetteer,' 1875 and 1876; on special duty in England, 1877-81; dir.-gen. of gazetteers, Oct., 1877; C.I.E., 1878; dir.-gen. of gazetteers and statistics, June, 1878; dir.-gen. of statistics, Dec., 1881; addl. member, govr.-gen.'s council, 1881, 1883, and 1885; prest. of the educn. commn., 1882-83; depd. to give evidence before the committee of the House of Commons on Indian railways, May, 1884; C.S.I., 1884; member of finance committee, Feb., 1886; K.C.S.I., Feb., 1887; retd., Sept., 1887; author of 'The Annals of Rural Bengal,' 1868; 'A Dictionary of the Non-Aryan Languages of India and High Asia,' 1868; memo. on the older records of the board of rev., N.W. Prov., 1870; 'The Indian Mussulmans,' 1871; 'The Province of Orissa,' 1872; 'Famine Aspects of Bengal Districts,' 1874; a collected edition of 'Essays on the External Policy of India,' by the late J. W. S. Wyllie, 1875; 'A Life of Lord Mayo,' 1875; 'A Statistical Account of Bengal,' 1875-77; a 'Statistical Account of Assam,' 1879; 'The Imperial Gazetteer of India,' 1881; 'The Indian Empire,' 1881; 'England's Work in India,' 1881; 'A Brief History of the Indian People,' &c., &c.

HURRY, ARNOLD EARDLEY, Indian C.S. (divl. judge, Punjab).—Educ. at the City of London Sch. and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1880; arrived, 25th Oct., 1882, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; from March, 1886, served in Burma as asst. commr. and dep. commr.; re-transfd., Dec., 1890; junr. sec. to govt., March, 1895; dep. commr., June, 1896; offg. divl. judge, March, 1897; confd., Oct., 1898.

HUSSEY, VICTOR EDWIN GROVE, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch. and Exeter Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1896; arrived, 28th Dec., 1898, and served in the N.W. Provs. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.

HUTCHESON, GEORGE, M.D., C.M., Col. Indian Med. Service (inspr.-gen. of hospls., N.W. Provs.).—Joined the service as asst. surgn., 1st Oct., 1866; served on gen. and mil. duty, and in 1870-71 as chemical analyst; on special duty, cholera enquiry, 1872-73; sanitary commr. and supt.-gen. of vaccn., N.W. Prov. and Oudh, May, 1889; on depn. to mil. duty, April-Oct., 1895; admn. med. officer, Central Provs., July, 1896; inspr.-gen. of civil hospls., N.W. Prov. and Oudh, May, 1899; author of 'Cholera, its Causes, and Mode of Dissemination,' and 'Cholera as a Preventible Disease.'

HUTCHINGS, FRANCIS SMITH (second class clerk, India Audit Office).—Clerk, 2nd class, 23rd June, 1879.

HUTCHINS, SIR PHILIP PERCEVAL, K.C.S.I.,

B.L., late Madras C.S. (member of the Council of India).—Apptd. to the Madras civil service from Haileybury, 1857; arrived, 27th Feb., 1858; served as asst. collr. and mag., dep. sec. to govt., sec. to board of examrs., and as judge of the court of small causes, Chittur; private sec. to govr. of Madras, Nov., 1863; registrar, high court, March, 1865; district and sessions judge, Madura, April, 1873; called to the bar (Inner Temple), June, 1875; member of rent law committee, Sept., 1882; acting sec., rev. dept., Dec., 1882; on special duty in connection with reorganization of jud. estabts., March, 1883; board of examrs., May, 1883; judge of the high court, Madras, Nov., 1883; fellow of Madras univ., March, 1884; member of govr.'s council, Dec., 1886; C.S.I., Jan., 1888; member of the council of the govr.-gen., Nov., 1888; K.C.S.I., May, 1891; retd., Nov., 1893; sec., jud. and public dept., India office, Dec., 1893, to July, 1898; member of Council of India, Aug., 1898.

HUTCHINSON, FREDERICK THOMAS, India Public Works Dept. (examr., public works accts., Bombay).—Joined the service, 25th March, 1871, as asst. comptr.; dep. comptr., May, 1874; examr., Sept., 1881; examr., public works accts., Bombay, May, 1890.

HUTCHINSON, HENRY NORTON, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Lancing Coll. and Clare Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1896; arrived 6th Dec., 1897, and served in the N.W. Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.

HUTCHINSON, JAMES BIRD, Lieut.-Col., I.S.C., late Punjab Commn.—Arrived, 26th May, 1864; from March, 1868, served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; district judge, Nov., 1884; dep. commr., Dec., 1884; served under govt. of India, Aug. to Oct., 1887; commr. and supt., Lahore, March, 1894; has publ. a 'Guide to Dalhousie,' and a gen. index of regulations and acts in force in the Punjab.

HUTCHINSON, SYDNEY HUTTON COOPER, A.M.I.C.E., Indian Telegraph Dept. (supt. of telegraphs).—Apptd., 30th Nov., 1871, as asst. supt.; supt., Bombay, Oct., 1887.

HUTTON, EDWARD, late Postal Dept., Govt. of India.—Joined the service as supt. of mails, Jabalpur, 10th Feb., 1858; supt., travelling post office, Nov., 1877; inspr., field force, Peshawar column, Feb., 1879; presidency postmr. at Calcutta, April, 1881; acted as postmr.-gen. at Lahore in 1890, at Bombay in 1891, and at Calcutta from April, 1892; retd., April, 1893.

HYDE, HENRY, Surgn.-Lieut.-Col., late Madras Med. Dept.—Arrived, 27th June, 1867; surgn., Oct., 1871; zillah surgn. and jail supt., April, 1872; civil surgn., Nov., 1884; retd., May, 1895.

HYNES, GEORGE JOHN, late Postal Dept., Govt. of India.—Entered the dept., 4th May, 1868; asst. dir.-gen. of the post office of India, Oct., 1880; dep. postmr.-gen., Burma, June, 1887; postmr.-gen., Punjab, Jan., 1891; dep. dir.-gen. of post office of India, July, 1891; offg. postmr.-gen., Bengal, March-Aug., 1894; retd. Jan., 1896.

IBBETSON, DENZIL CHARLES JELF, C.S.I., B.A., Indian C.S. (chief commr., Central Provs.).—Educ. at Coll. Sch., Adelaide, and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1868; arrived, 8th Dec., 1870,

and served in the Punjab as asst. commr. and settl. officer; dep. supt. of census, Punjab, Aug., 1881; compiler, 'Punjab Gazetteer,' March, 1883; offg. dir. of public instrn., March, 1884; dep. commr., April, 1885; offg. supt., Kapurthala state, Nov., 1885; offd. as 2nd finl. commr. in the Punjab in 1890; in charge of Gujranwala settl., May, 1891; member of Deccan agriculturists relief commn., 1891-92; on special duty connected with the cantonmt. act, 1893; offg. sec. to govt. of India, dept. of rev. and agric., July, 1894; promoted to commr., retaining his offg. appt., April, 1895; C.S.I., Jan., 1896; sec. to govt. of India, dept. of rev. and agric., Dec., 1896; chief commr., Central Provs., July, 1898; temp. member of govr.-gen.'s council, Dec., 1899; has publ. 'Census Report of the Punjab (1881)'; 'Outlines of Punjab Ethnography,' the Karnal settl. report, and contributions to the provl. gazetteers.

ILBERT, SIR COURTENAY PEREGRINE, K.C.S.I., C.I.E. (parly. counsel to the Treasury).—Educ. at Marlborough and Oxford; scholar, and afterwards fellow of Balliol Coll.; Hertford scholar, 1861; Ireland scholar, 1864; Craven scholar, 1864; Eldon law scholar, 1867; called to the bar at Lincoln's Inn, 7th June, 1869; counsel to educn. dept., 1878-82; law member of the council of the govr.-gen. of India, 1st May, 1882, and was prest. of the council, 3rd Feb. to 8th March, 1886; resigned, 13th Nov., 1886; vice-chancellor of Calcutta univ., 1885-86; asst. parly. counsel to the treasury, 1886; K.C.S.I., Jan., 1895; parly. counsel to the Treasury, Jan., 1899.

IMPEY, WILLIAM HENRY LOCKINGTON, Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Wellington Coll.; apptd. after exam. of 1876; arrived, 7th Nov., 1878, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; asst. commr., Naini Tal, Nov., 1886; under sec. to govt. of N.W. Prov. and Oudh, May, 1888; settl. officer, Oct., 1889; joint mag., April, 1892; sec. to govt., finl. dept., Aug., 1893; also member of legisl. council, Dec., 1893; mag. and collr., and acting sec. to govt., jud. dept., March, 1895; part author of Jhansi settl. report.

INAM ALI, Maulvi, B.A. (dist. judge, Punjab).—Apptd. asst. profr., govt. coll., Lahore, 31st Oct., 1887; probr. under native civil service rules, 4th Feb., 1888; asst. commr., April, 1891; dist. judge, Aug., 1895; has publ. in Urdu a transln. and an epitome of Hamilton's lectures on psychology.

INCH, GEORGE FREDERICK (2nd class clerk, Acctnt.-Gen.'s Dept., India Office).—Clerk, 2nd class, 6th Oct., 1879.

INGLIS, ERNEST, Major, I.S.C., (divl. judge, Punjab).—Arrived 27th March, 1877; in mil. employ till 1886; asst. commr., Punjab, Aug., 1886; dep. commr., Nov., 1896; divl. judge, Dec., 1898.

INGLIS, THOMAS, Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Wellington Coll., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1878; arrived, 27th Nov., 1880, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., May, 1890; junr. sec. to board of rev., July, 1892; mag. and collr., June, 1896.

INGLIS, WILLIAM ARBUTHNOT, India Public Works Dept. (suptg. engr.).—Trained at Cooper's Hill Coll.; apptd. asst. cngr., 1st Oct., 1874, and

employed on irrign. works in Bengal; exec. engr., May, 1883; offg. under sec., public works dept., Bengal, 1891-92; suptg. engr., July, 1896.

INGRAM, GERALD CONSTANTINE WINNINGTON, B.A., Indian C.S. (joint mag., N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Bromsgrove Sch., and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1892; arrived, 13th Dec., 1893, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr. and asst. commr.; joint mag., Dec., 1898.

INNES, CHARLES ALEXANDER, Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Madras).—Educ. at Merchant Taylors' Sch., and St. John's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1897; arrived 5th Dec., 1898, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.

INNES, JAMES JOHN McLEOD, Lieut.-Gen., V.C., R.E., late India Public Works Dept.—First commn. in Bengal engrs., 1848; served under the civil engr., Punjab, from 1852; asst. to chief engr., Oudh, Jan., 1857; served during the Mutiny (medal and two clasps, V.C.); exec. engr., Cawnpore, Dec., 1857; garrison engr., presidency, April, 1858; compt. and examr. of accts., Lahore, Feb., 1861; offg. acctnt.-gen., public works dept., 1865-66, and 1867-68; suptg. engr., Punjab, Oct., 1869; acctnt.-gen. and dep. ec., public works dept., June, 1870; chief engr., Sept., 1881, and dep. inspr.-gen., mil. works; inspr.-gen., 1882; ret'd., March, 1886.

INNES, LEWIS CHARLES, late Madras C.S.—Educ. at Charterhouse and Haileybury, and apptd. in 1844; served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag., sub-judge, and civil and sessions judge; judge of the high court, Madras, Feb., 1865, to Sept., 1883, and offd. on several occasions as chief justice; ret'd., 1883; fellow of the univ. of Madras, 1865-83; vice-chancellor, 1874-80.

IRELAND, WILLIAM DE COURCY, B.A., LL.D., late Burma Commn.—Joined the service as acctnt. in office of commr. of Pegu, April, 1859; asst. supt. of forests, Tounghoo, April, 1860; collr. of customs, Nov., 1860; from April, 1861, served in the commn. as asst. commr., town mag. and dep. commr.; acted also as inspr.-gen. of police; boundary officer, Akyab district, Sept., 1883; addl. sessions judge, Tenasserim, Jan., 1886; commr., Irawadi, July, 1886, and Arakan, Jan., 1890; ret'd., Dec., 1890.

IRVINE, GEORGE DUNCAN, late Madras C.S.—Educ. at Merchant Taylors' Sch., and St. John's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1861; served in Madras from May, 1865, as asst. collr. and mag., sub-collr. and joint mag., and acting collr. of sea customs at Madras; district and sessions judge, July, 1882; ret'd., June, 1891.

IRVINE, ROBERT HAMILTON GRANT, late Bengal Police Dept.—Joined the service as dep. mag. and dep. collr., Aug., 1861; apptd. asst. supt. of police, April, 1863; district supt., July, 1869; ret'd., Sept., 1891.

IRVINE, WILLIAM, late Bengal C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at St. Anne's Sch., Brixton, and King's Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1862; arrived, 12th Dec., 1863, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., and joint mag. and dep. collr.; mag. and collr., Dec., 1881; ret'd., 1888; author of 'Canal Rates versus Land Revenue,' 'The Rent Digest: or the Law Procedure relating to

Landlord and Tenant, Bengal,' 'Bangash Nawabs of Farukhabad,' and Ghazipur settlt. report.

IRVING, LEWIS ALLEN, Lieut.-Col., late Royal Army Med. Corps.—Med. officer to govr. of Bombay (Rt. Hon. Lord Reay), 1885 to 1890; ret'd., April, 1898.

IRWIN, ALFRED MACDONALD BULTEEL, Indian C.S. (commr., Burma).—Apptd. after exam. of 1874; arrived, 24th Nov., 1876, and served in the Punjab till June, 1877, and subsequently in Burma as asst. commr.; dep. commr., Aug., 1886; offd. as commr. in 1894, and from June, 1895; confd., May, 1898.

IRWIN, GEORGE ROBERT, B.A., Indian C.S. (Resident, Jaipur).—Educ. at Uppingham, and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1876; arrived, 16th Nov., 1878, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. commr.; asst. sec. to govt. of India, foreign dept., Dec., 1886; first asst. sec. for Berar to rest., Haidarabad, March, 1892; pol. agent, Jhalawar, April, 1893; resdt., Jaipur, April, 1898.

IRWIN, HENRY, C.I.E., M.I.C.E., late Madras Public Works Dept.—Joined the service as asst. engr., Central Prov., 1st Feb., 1868; exec. engr., Sept., 1872; supt. of works, Simla imperial circle, Oct., 1881; C.I.E., June, 1888; acting public works sec. to agents for Rajputana and Central India, Nov., 1888; transf. to Madras public works dept. as suptg. engr., and apptd. consulting architect to govt., Sept., 1889; ret'd. Jan., 1896.

IRWIN, HENRY CROSSLY, B.A., late Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Blackheath Proprietary Sch., St. Edmund's Hall, and Queen's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1869; arrived, 6th Nov., 1871, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. commr., joint mag. and dep. commr.; mag. and collr., Nov., 1889; acted as sec. to the board of rev. in 1893; offd. as dist. and sess. judge, April, 1895; ret'd., July, 1896; author of 'The Garden of India; or Chapters on Oudh History and Affairs.'

ISAACSON, STEPHEN HENRY HOLDEN (2nd class clerk, Registry and Record Dept., India Office).—Writer, India office, 15th April, 1869; clerk, 2nd class, Sept., 1878.

ISMAY, STANLEY, B.L., Indian C.S. (judl. commr., Central Prov.).—Educ. at Bromsgrove Gr. Sch.; apptd. after exam. of 1869; arrived, 14th Nov., 1871; served in the Central Prov. as asst. commr., and was on special duty in the Kaiahandi state, 1882; registrar, jud. commr.'s court, May, 1884; offg. inspr.-gen. of police and jails, 1885 and 1890; judge, small cause court, Jabalpur, Dec., 1889; dep. commr., Jan., 1890; addl. sessions judge, Jan., 1892; offg. jud. commr., Feb., 1895; divl. judge, June, 1895; iudl. commr., Dec., 1897; author of 'Rules for the Superintendence and Management of Jails in the Central Prov.' (1895).

IVENS, JOHN HENRY ANDERSON, India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at Cooper's Hill Coll.; apptd. to the public works dept. as asst. engr., Oct., 1877; exec. engr., Aug., 1891.

IVENS, THOMAS EDWARD, A.M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. asst. engr., 26th July, 1867, and posted to the Punjab; exec. engr., Sept., 1874; offg. under sec., Oct., 1886, and again, May, 1889; suptg. engr., Oct., 1892; ret'd. 1898.

IZAT, ALEXANDER, C.I.E., M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Joined the dept., June, 1863, as asst. engr., Haidarabad; exec. engr., March, 1868; transfed. to railway branch, 1870, and served on Nizam's, Holkar's, Dhond and Manmad, Kathiawar, and Bombay E. Deccan lines; suptg. engr., April, 1880; offg. under sec. to govt. of India, railway branch, Jan., 1883; services lent to Bengal and N.W. railway co. as agent and chief engr., Feb., 1883; ret'd., 1889; C.I.E., Dec., 1898.

JACKSON, ARCHIBALD (2nd class clerk, India Audit Office).—Clerk, 2nd class, 24th Dec., 1892.

JACKSON, ARTHUR MASON TIPPETTS, M.A., B.L., Indian C.S. (private sec. to govr. of Bombay).—Educ. at Westminster Sch., and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1885; arrived, 17th Dec., 1888, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; asst. collr. and inspr. of factories, Bombay, Sept., 1892; 2nd asst., July, 1895; under sec. to govt., rev. and finl. depts., Feb., 1897; 1st asst., May, 1898; private sec. to govr. of Bombay, Sept., 1898.

JACKSON, EDWIN JAMES, late Survey of India Dept.—Joined the service, 26th Sept., 1864; asst. supt., Nov., 1868; dep. supt., Oct., 1885; supt., cadastral survey, Burma, March, 1895; ret'd., Nov., 1899.

JACKSON, FRANK ERNEST, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Assam).—Educ. at the City of London Sch., and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1891; arrived, 17th Nov., 1893, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr. and sub-divl. officer; asst. commr., Assam, Feb., 1896.

JACKSON, FREEMAN HENRY, Lieut.-Col., I.S.C., late Pol. Dept., Bombay.—First commn., Bombay infantry, 1861; arrived, 10th Jan., 1862; from Jan., 1867, acted in charge of office of asst. rest., Amreli, and was apptd. to settle boundaries in E. Kathiawar, Jan., 1868; acting asst. rest., Dwarika, and commandant of the Gaekwar's battalion, March, 1869; asst. rest., Baroda, in charge Okhamandal, July, 1874; on duty in Baroda, Jan., 1875; extra asst. gen. supt., thagi and dakaiti dept., June, 1875; from May, 1876, served as asst. agent to the govr.-gen., Baroda, and from Feb., 1879, as supt. of the Gaekwar's contingent, Kathiawar, asst. in charge of Amreli Mahals, and supt. for the improvement of the breed of horses in Kathiawar; offd. for various periods as rest. and govr.-gen.'s agent, Baroda; pol. supt. of Palanpur, Aug., 1895; returned to Europe, Dec., 1897.

JACKSON, HENRY WYCKHAM, Police Dept., Punjab (dep. inspr.-gen. of police).—Entered the service as asst. dist. supt., May, 1869; dist. supt., April, 1876; dep. inspr.-gen., July, 1898.

JACKSON, HUGH MILBOURNE, Major, R.E., late Survey of India Dept.—First commn., 19th June, 1877; entered the dept. as asst. supt., June, 1883; dep. supt., April, 1889; served in the Burma expdn., 1886, the first S. Shan expdn., 1886-87, and with the N. Karen column, 1888-89.

JACKSON, WILLIAM GRIERSON, Indian C.S. (inspr.-gen. of registrn. and commr. of excise, N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Manchester Gr. Sch., and Univ. of London; apptd. after exam. of 1872; arrived, 12th Nov., 1874, and

served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., and in British Burma as asst. commr.; dep. supt. of family domains of Maharaja of Benares, March, 1882; joint mag., Nov., 1887; dep. commr., Aug., 1891; mag. and collr., Sept., 1892; inspr.-gen. of registrn. and commr. of excise and stamps, March, 1897; author of Mirzapur volume of N.W. Prov. Gazetteer.

JACOB, EDWARD FOUNTAINE, C.I.E., A.M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept. (traffic supt., N.W. railway).—Trained at Cooper's Hill Coll.; joined the service, 1st Oct., 1876, as asst. engr.; district traffic supt., Feb., 1880; attached to public works secretariat, govt. of India, April, 1888; dep. traffic supt., N.W. railway, May, 1890; traffic supt., May, 1892; C.I.E., May, 1898.

JACOB, GEORGE, Indian C.S. (judge and sessions judge, Bombay).—Educ. at Bedford Gr. Sch., and Highgate Gr. Sch.; apptd. after exam. of 1877; arrived, 21st Nov., 1879, and served as asst. collr. and mag.; from May, 1883, served as asst. judge and sessions judge, and as asst. agent for sardars in the Deccan; joint judge and sessions judge, Nov., 1886; acting registrar, high court, appellate side, Feb., 1888; 1st asst. judge, Nov., 1891; judge and sessions judge, March, 1894.

JACOB, HERBERT BRUCE, Col. I.S.C., late Jud. Dept., Bombay.—First commn., Dec., 1857; served from 26th June, 1869, in the edulc. dept., Central Prov., and was apptd. inspr. of schools, April, 1871; acting inspr.-gen. of educn., 1882-84; cantonmt. mag., Karachi, 1889-92; ret'd. to Europe, Dec., 1895.

JACOB, LIONEL MONTAGUE, India Public Works Dept. (supg. engr.).—Trained at Cooper's Hill Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1875, and employed on irrign. works in the Punjab; exec. engr., May, 1883; under sec. to govt., irrign. branch, Nov., 1894; supg. engr., Jan., 1899.

JACOB, SAMUEL SWINTON, C.I.E., A.I.C.E., Col., I.S.C., late India Public Works Dept.—Apptd., 17th Nov., 1862, and posted to Bombay; services lent to Jaipur state, July, 1867; exec. engr., Sept., 1870; public works sec. to agent in Rajputana, 1886, 1887, and 1889; C.I.E., May, 1890; suptg. engr., Jan., 1893; services specially retained, under Jaipur state, Dec., 1896; sec. to govt. of Punjab, public works dept., Nov., 1897; returned to Europe, 1898.

JACOB, SYDNEY LONG, Col., R.E., India Public Works Dept. (chief engr.).—Joined the service, 13th January, 1869, and served on irrign. works in Bombay and the Punjab as asst. engr. and exec. engr.; served through the Afghan war, 1878-79 (medal); suptg. engr., Oct., 1890; chief engr., Feb., 1898.

JAMES, ARTHUR HUTTON, Police Dept., Bengal (commr. of police, Calcutta).—Joined the service, 16th Feb., 1863, as asst. supt. of police, Assam; also asst. commr., Nov., 1866; district supt., Oct., 1873; transfed. to Bengal, Dec., 1875; asst. inspr.-gen., railway police, Nov., 1884; commr. of police, Calcutta, May, 1897.

JAMES, CHARLES STREATFIELD, M.I.C.E., Indian Telegraph Dept. (supt. of telegraphs).—Trained at Roy. Indian Engrg. Coll.; apptd. asst. supt., Jan., 1879; supt., Jan., 1899.

JAMES, HENRY EVAN MURCHISON, C.S.I., Indian C.S. (commr. in Sind).—Educ. at the Cathedral Sch., Carlisle, and Durham Gr. Sch.; apptd. after exam. of 1864; arrived, 14th Dec., 1865, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and as branch inspr.-gen. of registrn., and educ. inspr. in Sind; under sec. to govt. in the rev., fin., and gen. depts., Nov., 1875; postmr.-gen., Bombay, Nov., 1877; postmr.-gen., Bengal, March, 1882; acted as dir.-gen. of the post office in India, 1883-85; dep. dir.-gen., Nov., 1885; senr. collr. and collr. and mag., Ahmadabad, April, 1888; commr., June, 1890; commr. in Sind, Nov., 1892; member of govr.-gen.'s legisl. council, Feb.-March, 1895, Dec., 1896-March, 1897, and Jan.-March, 1898; C.S.I., Jan., 1898; offg. member of council, July, 1898, and April, 1899.

JAMESON, ARTHUR SAMPSON, M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept. (locomotive supt.).—Joined the service, 1st Jan., 1886, and served in the locomotive dept., N.W. railway; transfed. to E. Bengal state railway, Aug., 1888; district locomotive supt., July, 1889; locomotive supt., Oct., 1890.

JAMESON, ROBERT, M.D., Brigade-Surgn.-Lieut.-Col., late Sanitary Dept., N.W. Prov. and Oudh.—Joined the Bengal med. service, Oct., 1865; arrived, 11th April, 1866, and was employed on civil and mil. duty; in 1868 was in med. charge at Calcutta of troops and coolies, en route to Abyssinia; served in Abyssinia with 12th Bengal cavalry; served in Bombay and Punjab, 1868 and 1869, and in the N.W. Prov. from 1870 on civil and jail duty dep. sanitary commr., Nov., 1886; retd., May, 1896.

JAQUET, ROBERT GLOVER (senr. clerk, Acctnt.-Gen.'s Dept., India Office).—Junr. clerk, 11th May, 1877; senr. clerk, April, 1897.

JARDINE, SIR JOHN, K.C.I.E., late Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Apptd. after exam. of 1863; arrived, 30th Oct., 1864, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and asst. judge and sessions judge; acting under sec. to govt., pol., &c., depts., June, 1869; from May, 1871, served in Kathiawar as pol. asst.; also jud. asst., June, 1873; acting registrar of the high court, Bombay, Oct., 1873; sec. to the Baroda commn. for the trial of the Gaekwar, 1875; sec. to the delegates of the govt. of India in the negotiations for a commercial treaty with Portugal, 1877, also acting as dep. sec. to govt. of India, legisl. dept.; acting sec. to govt. of Bombay, pol., &c., depts., Nov., 1877; from Feb., 1878, served in British Burma as jud. commr.; prest. of the educ. syndicate, Sept., 1881; acting chief sec. to govt. of Bombay, Oct., 1885; judge of the high court, Bombay, Nov., 1885; acted as chief justice in 1895; retd., Feb., 1897; K.C.I.E., June, 1897; author of 'Notes on Buddhist Law'; editor of Moung Tet Pyo's Customary Law of the Chin Tribe, also of a reprint of Dr. Tandy's transl. of Father Sangermano's description of the Burman Empire, and a compilation of printed judgments of the jud. commr. of British Burma; wrote a preface to Dr. Forchhammer's 'King Wagaru's Dhamma-Sattham.'

JARDINE, WILLIAM ELLIS, Indian C.S. (pol. asst., Indore).—Educ. at Fettes Coll., Edinburgh, and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd.

after exam. of 1886; arrived, 20th Feb., 1889, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; second asst. to rest., Haidarabad, Nov., 1893; head asst. and mag., Bellary, March, 1896; pol. asst., Haidarabad, Jan., 1897; ditto, Indore, May, 1898; and asst. to the agent to the govr.-gen. in Central India.

JARRETT, HENRY SULLIVAN, C.I.E., Col., I.S.C., late Legisl. Dept., Govt. of India.—First commn., Dec., 1856; served during the Mutiny (medal); also in the Mahsud Waziri expdn., 1860 (medal and clasp), and the 2nd Yusufzai expdn., 1863 (clasp); sec. to board of examrs., Fort William, March, 1871; in addn., asst. sec., legisl. dept., govt. of India, from Aug., 1875; reverted to mil. dept., June, 1894; C.I.E., Jan., 1895.

JEFFERY, THADDEUS JAMES PASSMORE, India Public Works Dept. (suptg. engr.).—Apptd. asst. engr., 15th July, 1869, and employed on irrign. works in Bombay and the Punjab; exec. engr., Feb., 1879; suptg. engr., Oct., 1894.

JEFFREYS, WILLIAM, Col., R.E., late India Public Works Dept.—First commn., 8th June, 1854; arrived, 24th Jan., 1856, and served in the irrign. branch, Punjab, and in the N.W. Prov.; on duty in the public works secretariat, govt. of India, 1864-65; transfed. to Bengal, and depd. to investigate a project of irrign. and navigation for the river Gandak, Oct., 1867; offg. asst. to chief engr., and asst. sec. to govt., N.W. Prov., May, 1869; suptg. engr., April, 1872; acting chief engr. and joint sec., irrign. branch, March, 1887; retd., Nov., 1887.

JEFFRIES, FRANCIS JOSEPH, B.A., LL.B., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Queen Mary's Sch., Walsall, and Caius Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived, 7th Dec., 1896, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

JEHANGIR DÖSABHAI FRAMJI, B.L., Customs Dept., Bombay (asst. collr. of customs).—Joined the service April, 1879; asst. collr. of customs, Feb., 1881.

JEHANGIR KAIKHASRO NAVROJI KABRAJI, Statutory C.S. (1st asst., Bombay).—Apptd. prob. under native civil service rules, 14th Jan., 1888; asst. collr. and mag., March, 1888; confd., 24th Feb., 1890; 2nd asst., May, 1895; 1st asst., July, 1897.

JEHANGIR PĒSTANJI, C.I.E., Khan Bahadur, late Bombay Uncov. Service.—Joined the service as asst. master, Elphinstone instn., Bombay, 10th Oct., 1853; served in the settlt. dept. from April, 1857; 1st grade dep. collr., Aug., 1869; C.I.E., Jan., 1882; talukdari settlt. officer, May, 1889; retd., 1889.

JENKINS, JOHN LEWIS, B.A., Indian C.S. (junr. collr. and collr. of salt rev., Bombay).—Educ. at Bristol Gr. Sch., and Wadham Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1877; arrived, 15th Jan., 1880, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and from June, 1881, in Sind; 3rd asst. collr. in Sind, June, 1885; manager of encumbered estates in Sind, and educ. inspr., Sept., 1885; 2nd asst. collr., May, 1889; 1st asst., Feb., 1892; asst. commr. in Sind, and Sindi translr. to govt., March, 1892; junr. collr. and collr. of salt rev., Oct., 1896.

JENKINS, SIR LAWRENCE HUGH, Knt., B.L. (chief justice, Bombay high court).—

Appdt. judge, high court, Calcutta, April, 1896; chief justice, high court, Bombay, April, 1899; Knt., Aug., 1899.

JENKINS, RICHARD ARTHUR, Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Madras).—Educ. at Highgate Sch., Brasenose Coll., Oxford, and Univ. Coll., London; appdt. after exam. of 1897; arrived 21st Nov., 1898, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.

JENKINS, ROBERT GEDOIN, Col., I.S.C., late Madras Police Dept.—Ensign, Madras infantry, Dec., 1856; arrived, 8th Feb., 1857; served in the Madras police from July, 1863, and was promoted to supt., 1872; reverted to mil. duty, 1890.

JENKINS, THOMAS MORRIS, Lieut.-Col., I.S.C., late Burma Commn.—Ensign, 2nd March, 1866; served in the Burma commn. as asst. commr. from 28th April, 1874; acted as port officer and collr. of customs, Bassein, in July, 1881; settlt. officer, 1884-85; dep. commr., Aug., 1886; ret'd., March, 1899.

JENKINSON, SIR EDWARD GEORGE, K.C.B., late Bengal C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Harrow and Haileybury; appdt. in 1856, and served in the N.W. Prov. as joint mag. and dep. collr. of Benares, and afterwards of Farukhabad; served through the Mutiny (medal); also as settlt. officer, mag. and collr., commr. of rev. in Jhansi, and commr. in Oudh; ret'd., 1880; was private sec. to Earl Spencer when lord-lieut. of Ireland, and asst. under sec. for police and crime, 1882-85; C.B. in 1883, and K.C.B. in 1888.

JENNINGS, ROBERT HENRY, Lieut.-Col., R.E., Foreign Dept., Govt. of India (pol. agent, Malwa).—First commn., 12th Sept., 1872; arrived, 1st Dec., 1875, and was employed as asst. engr. and in various acting appts. in the Punjab and Baluchistan; served through Afghan war, 1879-80, and the operations against the Maris, 1880; on boundary settlt. duty in Central India, 1883-84; dep. asst. quartermaster-gen., Simla, July, 1885; district staff officer, Meerut, Sept., 1888; dep. asst. adjutant-gen., Meerut, April, 1890; exec. engr., mil. works dept., June, 1890; acting asst. pol. agent and consul, Bassora, Feb.-Aug., 1892; pol. officer with Sardar Ayub Khan, Feb., 1893; offg. pol. agent, Alwar, April, 1896; confid., Oct., 1896; pol. agent, Malwa, May, 1897; author of an account of a journey through W. Baluchistan, Persia, &c., and of contributions to Persia and Baluchistan gazetteers.

JERVOISE, ALAN ARTHUR CLARKE, late Bombay C.S.—Appdt. from Haileybury, 1856; arrived, 23rd March, 1857, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., pol. agent and collr. of stamp rev., and acting dep. commr. of customs and opium; junr. collr. and mag., Sept., 1877; senr. collr. and mag., March, 1879; ret'd., April, 1887.

JEWETT, THOMAS HOPE, India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at Calcutta Civil Engrg. Coll.; appdt. 22nd May, 1872, and posted to mil. works; on famine duty in Mysore, 1877-78; transfed. to Assam, Sept., 1880; exec. engr., April, 1886.

JOGESH CHANDRA MITTER, B.L., Bengal Judicial Dept. (subordinate judge).—Dep. mag. and dep. collr., July, 1868; munsi, April, 1870; subordinate judge, July, 1882.

JOHNS, WILLIAM ARTHUR, A.M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept.—Trained at the Royal India Engrg. Coll.; appdt. asst. engr., Sept., 1880, and posted to state railways; exec. engr., Nov., 1891.

JOHNSON, SIR ALLEN BAYARD, K.C.B., Gen., I.S.C., late Mil. Dept., India Office.—Educ. at Winchester; joined the service (infantry), July, 1846; appdt. to 4th Sikhs as volunteer for service in Burma (medal and clasp for Pegu); in civil employ in Punjab, 1854; served in the Mutiny in 1858 (medal and clasp); asst. adjutant-gen., 1861; mil. dept., Calcutta, 1862; asst. sec. to govt. of India in the mil. dept., 1875; dep. mil. sec., 1876; sec. in the mil. dept., India office, from Jan., 1877; temporarily employed as mil. sec. to the govt. of India, 1879-81; C.B., 1881; ret'd. from India office and was made K.C.B., May, 1889.

JOHNSON, CHRISTOPHER THOMAS HEATON, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Madras).—Educ. at Sedbergh and Wadham Coll., Oxford; appdt. after exam. of 1896; arrived, 6th Dec., 1897, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.

JOHNSON, The Most Rev. EDWARD RALPH, D.D., LL.D.—Educ. at Rugby, and Wadham Coll., Oxford; ordained, 1851; vicar of Northenden, Cheshire, 1866-76; archdeacon of Chester, 1871-76; appdt. bishop of Calcutta and metropolitan of India, 14th Dec., 1876, resigned May, 1898.

JOHNSON, EVANS CHARLES, late Madras C.S.—Educ. at Cheltenham Coll., and Trinity Coll., Dublin; appdt. after exam. of 1865; arrived, 29th Nov., 1867, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; on special duty, Rump disturbances, 1879-80; sub-collr. and joint mag., S. Arcot, Jan., 1883; collr. and mag., May, 1886; district and sessions judge, Oct., 1886; collr. and mag. and agent, Ganjam, May, 1890; ret'd., March, 1893.

JOHNSON, JOHN (second-class clerk, Acctnt.-Gen.'s Dept., India Office).—Clerk, 2nd class, 17th Feb., 1872; junr. clerk, June, 1882.

JOHNSON, THOMAS GEORGE, Major, I.S.C. (dep. commr., Burma).—First commn., Aug., 1877; employed in Burma as asst. commr., March, 1884; dep. commr., March, 1891.

JOHNSTON, The Ven. CHARLES FRANCIS HARDING, M.A., late Ecclesi. Dept., Bombay.—educ. at Christ's Coll., Cambridge; junr. chaplain, 13th Feb., 1869; senr. chaplain, Feb., 1879; archdeacon of Bombay, Oct., 1888; ret'd., Oct., 1890.

JOHNSTON, DAVID LANDALE, Indian C.S. (joint mag., N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Boston Gr. Sch., and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; appdt. after exam. of 1889; arrived, 19th Nov., 1891, and served as asst. commr. and asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., Feb., 1897.

JOHNSTON, FREDERICK WILLIAM, B.A., LL.B., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at Glasgow Univ. and Trinity Hall, Cambridge; appdt. after exam. of 1895; arrived, 17th Dec., 1896, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

JOHNSTON, HENRY JOSEPH, India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at Cooper's Hill Coll.; appdt. asst. engr., Sept., 1880, and posted to the Punjab; exec. engr., Jan., 1894.

JOHNSTON, JAMES, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Royal Univ. of Ireland and Merton Coll. Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1896; arrived, 19th Nov., 1897, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

JOHNSTON, JAMES LEONARD, B.L., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Marlborough and Trin. Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived, 24th Feb., 1897, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.

JOHNSTON, JOHN CHARLES, A.M.I.C.E., Madras Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Joined the service, 11th Feb., 1873, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., Jan., 1885.

JOHNSTON, JOHN LEECH, B.A., late Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at the Royal Sch., Raphoe, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1865; arrived, 26th Nov., 1867, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and asst. judge; judge and sessions judge, Jan., 1884; jud. commr. and judge of sadar court in Sind, May, 1895; ret'd., May, 1898.

JOHNSTONE, DONALD CAMPBELL, Indian C.S. (divl. judge, Punjab).—Educ. at Edinburgh Acad.; apptd. after exam. of 1877; arrived, 10th Nov., 1879, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr., and in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; junr. sec. to finl. commr., Punjab, Nov., 1888; district judge, Dec., 1891; divl. judge, Aug., 1895.

JOHNSTONE, FREDERICK JOHN, C.I.E., M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Served the Central Prov., from July, 1861, and was apptd. asst. to the chief engr., Aug., 1873; offg. comptr. of public works accts., 1875; transfld. temporarily to Punjab, Oct., 1879; suptg. engr., Nov., 1880; posted to British Burma, Jan., 1881; transfld. to Assam as sec. in the public works dept., Jan., 1884; chief engr., June, 1888; chief engr. and joint sec. to the govt. of Bengal, June, 1891; sec., March, 1892; fellow of Calcutta univ., 1893; ret'd., Jan., 1896; C.I.E., Jan., 1896.

JOHNSTONE, JAMES WILLIAM HOPE, Col., I.S.C., late Punjab Comm.—Arrived, 4th Feb., 1853; from March, 1858, served in the Punjab as asst. commr., and dep. commr.; offd. as commr. in 1880 and 1883; civil and sessions judge, Peshawar, Sept., 1881; divl. judge, Nov., 1884; ret'd., Feb., 1886.

JOLL, HENRY, late India Public Works Dept.—Joined the service, 26th July, 1867, and served as asst. engr. and exec. engr.; suptg. engr., Nov., 1890; ret'd., March, 1892.

JOLLIFFE, EDWIN WILLIAM, Registry and Record Dept., India Office.—Clerk, 2nd class, 2nd April, 1885.

JONES, JOHN ALFRED, M.I.C.E. (sanitary engr., Madras).—Joined the service as exec. engr., public works dept., Madras municipal commn., Jan., 1877; acted as prest. of the commn. in 1886 and 1888; apptd. sanitary engr. to govt. of Madras, April, 1890; author of 'Switches and Crossings,' and of a manual for district and municipal boards.

JONES, OSWALD ROUTH (chief presidency mag., Madras).—Joined the service, 3rd Sept., 1870, under the mil. dept.; served subsequently in Natal, Mauritius, and in England; in April, 1879, was apptd. to Madras as asst. supt. of police; asst. inspr.-gen., Aug., 1886; supt., Aug., 1889; chief presidency mag., March, 1891.

JONES, RICHARD GODFREY, Major, I.S.C.,

Foreign Dept., Govt. of India.—First commn., 13th June, 1874; served with 67th Foot till Jan., 1878; entered the Staff Corps, Jan., 1878; inspg. officer, Mysore Imperial Troops, July, 1890; mil. sec. to the rest., Haidarabad, March, 1895; mil. asst. to Mysore govt., 1897.

JONES, THOMAS, B.L., late Bengal Jud. Dept.—Asst. sec., govt. of Bengal, 7th April, 1866; clerk and offg. 5th judge, Calcutta court of small causes, Jan., 1873; registrar and chief ministerial officer of the court, June, 1882; 4th judge, Oct., 1884; and 3rd judge, Oct., 1889; ret'd., 1892.

JONES, WILLIAM BRITTAIN, C.S.I., late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1855; arrived, 15th Sept., 1856, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Punjab as asst. mag. and asst. commr.; from April, 1865, served in the Central Prov. as dep. commr., sec. to the chief commr., and acting commr. in various divns.; on special duty for drawing up land rev. and tenancy bills for Central Prov., Sept., 1874; commr., Haidarabad assigned districts, April, 1877; rest., Haidarabad, June, 1882; chief commr., Central Prov., May, 1883; ret'd., Jan., 1885.

JONES, WILLIAM CHARLES NIGEL, M.I.E.E. (supt., India Telegraph Dept.).—Apptd. asst. supt., 24th Feb., 1870; in charge, Jowaki field telegraphs, 1877-78 (medal); also employed in connection with Kuram valley field force; supt., Aug., 1887; personal asst. to dir.-gen., 1889-90.

JONES, WILLIAM RICHARD SUMPTION, late India Public Works Dept.—Dep. supt., Rurki workshops, Nov., 1867; joined the public works dept., Feb., 1873, as exec. engr., N.W. Prov.; transfld. to Rajputana state railways, April, 1879, as dep. locomotive supt.; carriage and wagon supt., Ajmir, January, 1880; ret'd., Dec., 1893.

JONES-BATEMAN, HERBERT BURLETON, Indian C.S. (district and sessions judge, N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Marlborough; apptd. after exam. of 1874; arrived, 16th Oct., 1876, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. commr. and joint mag.; small cause court judge, March, 1892; district and sessions judge, April, 1892.

JOPP, CHARLES HENRY, B.A., Indian C.S. (judge and sessions judge, Bombay).—Educ. at Winchester, and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1870; arrived, 19th Oct., 1873, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; joint judge and sessions judge, Ahmadabad, April, 1892; registrar, high court, Bombay, May, 1893; acting special judge under Deccan agriculturists relief act, Nov., 1893; judge and sessions judge, Ahmednagar, January, 1897.

JOPP, JOHN, C.B., Col., late Pol. Dept., Bombay.—First commn., 20th Oct., 1856; served at capture of fort of Sheik Othman, near Aden, in 1858, in the Abyssinian war of 1868 (medal), and in the Afghan war, 1879-80 (medal); dep. quartermaster-gen., Bombay, May, 1888; pol. rest., &c., Aden, May, 1890; C.B., May, 1894; reverted to mil. dept., May, 1895.

JOSCELYNE, DOUGLAS, India Public Works Dept. (suptg. engr.).—Apptd. asst. engr., 11th Aug., 1870, and posted to Rajputana; employed in Madras on famine relief, 1878-79; exec. engr., Jan., 1880; transfld. to Burma as asst. sec. in the public works secretariat, July, 1888; under sec., Nov., 1889, to March, 1890; suptg. engr., August, 1895.

JOSEPH, EUSTACE ALEXANDER ACWORTH, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at Winchester and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1897; arrived 24th Nov., 1898, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

JOSEPH, GEORGE JOHN, India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at Calcutta Civil Engrg. Coll.; apptd., 23rd May, 1872, and posted to the N.W. Prov. and Oudh; exec. engr., May, 1883.

JOSEPH, HUGH GORE, M.A., Indian C.S. (district and sessions judge, Madras).—Educ. at the Charterhouse, and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1875; arrived, 7th Nov., 1877, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag., and in Burma (1886-87) as dep. commr.; received Burma war medal and clasp; returned to Madras as head asst.; sub-collr. and joint mag., Feb., 1890; district and sessions judge, Jan., 1894.

JOUBERT, CHARLES HENRY, M.B., Lieut.-Col., Indian Med. Service (prof., Calcutta Med. Coll.).—Joined the service, 30th March, 1872, and was in mil. employ until 1874; served as civil surgn., rest. surgn., presidency hosp., offg. supt. of emigration, offg. health officer for Calcutta, and offg. sanitary commr. for Bengal; profir., med. coll., June, 1890; fellow of Calcutta univ., Jan., 1893.

JOWERS, HAROLD, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Central Provs.).—Educ. at Shrewsbury, and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1889; arrived, 15th Nov., 1891, and served in the Central Prov. as asst. commr.

JOYCE, ALFRED, M.I.C.E., Madras Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1874, as asst. engr., and was employed in the N.W. Prov.; transf. to Madras, April, 1883; exec. engr., Jan., 1886.

JOYNER, ROBERT BATSON, C.I.E., M.I.C.E., late Bombay Public Works Dept.—Joined the service, 8th Oct., 1868, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., Jan., 1879; acted as sanitary engr. in 1892; supt. engr., May, 1894; C.I.E., January, 1898; ret'd., Nov., 1898.

JWALA PRASAD, Kunwar, B.A., Statutory C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Apptd. a prob. under the native civil service rules, Dec., 1884; confd., as asst. mag. and collr., Feb., 1887; asst. commr., Nov., 1891; joint mag., Feb., 1896; addl. judge, Dec., 1896; dist. and sess. judge, Feb., 1898; has transld. Sir Alfred Lyall's 'Asiatic Studies' into Hindustani.

JWALA PRASAD, Pandit, M.A., Statutory C.S. (joint mag., N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Apptd. a prob. under native civil service rules, July, 1886; confd., 12th March, 1889, as asst. commr.; joint mag., Nov., 1893.

JYOTSANATH GHOSAL, Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Bombay).—Educ. at Metropolitan Coll., Calcutta; Calcutta Univ., and Univ. Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1894; arrived, 25th Nov., 1895; and served in Bombay as asst. coll. and mag.

KALI PRASANNA MUKERJI, B.A., B.L., late Bengal Jud. Dept.—Joined the service, 23rd Feb., 1866, as munsif; subord. judge, Feb., 1879; ret'd., Feb., 1898.

KAVASJEE JAMASJEE BADSHAH, Statutory C.S. (comptr., Post Office, Bengal).—Educ. at Elphinstone High Sch., Bombay Univ., and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of

1877; arrived, 12th Nov., 1879, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; postmr.-gen., N.W. Prov. and Oudh, Feb., 1887; dep. dir.-gen. of post office, July, 1896; comptr., post office, April, 1897.

KAYE, WILLIAM, late Bengal C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Apptd. from Haileybury, 1853; arrived, 8th April, 1858; served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., joint mag. and dep. collr., and mag. and collr.; commr. of Agra, July, 1885, and of Rohilkhand, Nov., 1889; on special duty in connection with the police re-organisation commn., 1890-61; junr. member of board of rev., Nov., 1891; ret'd., April, 1893.

KAY-SHUTTLEWORTH, RIGHT HON. SIR UGHTRED JAMES, Bart. M.P.—Educ. at Harrow, and London Univ.; M.P. for Hastings, 1869-80; served for two years on the London sch. board, and was also a member of the royal commn. on reformatory and industrial schools; from 1885, M.P. for the Clitheroe division of N.E. Lancashire; apptd. under sec. of state for India in Feb., 1886, and shortly afterwards chancellor of the duchy of Lancaster; parly. sec., admiralty, Aug., 1892, to Aug., 1895; was for some time vice-prest. of Univ. Coll., London.

KEALY, EDWARD HERBERT, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Felsted, and Univ. Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1896; arrived, 9th Dec., 1897, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

KEANE, MICHAEL, Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Blackrock Coll., Dublin, and Univ. Coll., London; appointed after exam. of 1897; arrived 21st Nov., 1898, and served in N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr.

KEATINGE, GERALD FRANCIS, Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Bombay).—Educ. at Sherborne Sch. and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1893; arrived, 10th Dec., 1894, and served in Bombay, as asst. collr. and mag.; acting dist. supt. of police, Kanara, Sept.-Dec., 1897, and May-June, 1898.

KEATINGE, RICHARD HARTE, V.C., C.S.I., Gen., I.S.C.—First commn., Bombay artillery, June, 1842; from 1847 was employed in civil duties on the N.W. frontier, and was apptd. asst. supt., Nimar, Dec., 1851; served in the Mutiny (medal and clasp and V.C.), including the pursuit of Tantia Topi, 1858-59; pol. agent, Nimar, 1860; pol. agent, Gwalior, and subsequently in Kathiawar, 1862; served in Kathiawar against the rebel Waghirs in 1865; agent to the govr.-gen. in Rajputana, 1868-74; 1st ex-officio chief commr. of Ajmir, 1871-74; chief commr. of Assam, Feb., 1874, to June, 1878.

KEDAR NATH MOZUMDAR, B.A., B.L. (subord. judge, Bengal).—Served in the subord. jud. dept. from 16th Dec., 1865, and was apptd. subord. judge, Jan., 1879.

KEDAR NATH ROY, M.A., B.L., Statutory C.S. (joint mag. and collr., Bengal).—From 4th April, 1878, served as munsif, and on 27th July, 1888, was apptd. to the statutory civil service as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., Oct., 1893; joint mag. and collr., Oct., 1896; author of three legal works.

KEEFER, WILLIAM NAPIER, Surgn.-Major, late Indian Med. Dept.—Asst. surgn., Bengal med. establ., 1st Oct., 1869; served in the

Lushai campaign, 1871-72 (medal and clasp), the Duffla expdn., 1874-75, the Jowaki campaign, 1877-78 (clasp), the Afghan campaign, 1878-80 (medal and clasp), the Zaimusht expdn., 1879, and the Egyptian campaign, 1882 (medal and clasp, bronze star, and 4th class Osmanieh); senr. med. officer, Port Blair, April 1884; retd., Dec., 1880.

KEEGAN, DENNIS FRANCIS, M.D., Brigade-Surgn.-Lieut.-Col., late Indian Med. Service.—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Dublin; joined the Bengal med. service in 1866; acted as residency surgn., Indore, 1877-78; served in the Afghan war, 1879-80 (medal and clasp, bronze star); apptd. residency surgn., Indore, and civil med. officer, Central India, June, 1881; retd., April, 1894; author of several articles and papers on med. subjects.

KEELAN, BERNARD CALLAN, Surgn.-Major, L.K. & Q.C.P., M.R.C.S., late Med. Dept., Bombay.—Joined the Bombay med. service, 1st April, 1867; served under the mil. dept., and also as civil surgn. and supt. of jail at various stations; took part in the Abyssinian campaign (medal); civil surgn., Sukkur, Jan., 1881; civil surgn., &c., Haidarabad, April, 1884; retd., March, 1897.

KEELAN, EUGENE JOHN, A.M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Trained at Thomason Coll.; apptd., 3rd Oct., 1868, and employed on irrign. works in Oudh; transf. to state railways, Nov., 1879; exec. engr., Oct., 1880; retd., 1896.

KEENE, CHARLES JAMES, India Public Works Dept. (dist. traffic supt.).—Joined the service, 29th Dec., 1874, as asst. traffic supt., Punjab N. state railway; served also on the N. Bengal state railway, the N.W. and other railways; district traffic supt., 1890.

KEENE, HENRY G., C.I.E., M.A. (Hon.), late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at Rugby, and Oxford; apptd. from Haileybury, 1847; arrived in India, 3rd Oct., 1847; served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., asst. supt. of Dehra Dun, and joint mag. and dep. collr.; supt., Dehra Dun, May, 1856; mag. and collr., April, 1860; civil and sessions judge, April, 1867; offd. as commr. of Agra in 1872 and 1875, and of Allahabad in 1874; retd., 1883; fellow of the Calcutta univ.; author of 'Indian Administration,' 1867; 'Fall of the Mughal Empire,' 1876; 'History of Hindustan,' 1885; 'Life of Madhava Rao Sindhia,' 1892; 'History of India for the use of Students,' 1893; 'A Servant of John Company,' 1897; and numerous contributions to the periodical press.

KEENE, HENRY GEORGE HARRIS, Finl. Dept., Govt. of India (comptroller, Haidarabad).—Joined the dept., 14th March, 1874, as asst. acctnt.-gen., asst. compt. - gen., and asst. compt., Burma; compt., Haidarabad, March-July, 1886, and from Oct., 1893.

KEESS, JAMES, M.D., M.R.C.P., L.M., M.R.C.S., Dep. Surgn.-Gen., late Madras Med. Dept.—Served in the subord. med. dept. from 11th Sept., 1848, to May, 1855; asst. surgn., 4th Aug., 1856; from May, 1862, served as civil surgn. and jail supt., Karnul, fort surgn., Madras, profr. in the med. coll., med. inspr. of seamen, &c.; fellow of Madras univ., 1882; principal and profr. of medicine in the med. coll., and senr. physician, gen. hosp., Aug., 1883; retd., Jan., 1887; author of 'Syllabus of

Medicine for use of Medical Students,' 'Notes on Zoology and Comparative Anatomy,' 'History of the Madras Medical College,' &c.

KEESS, WALTER, Madras Educ. Dept.—Apptd. principal, Coll. of Agric., Saidapet, Jan., 1897.

KEITH, STANLEY, Finl. Dept., India Office.—Educ. at Lancing Coll.; writer, India office, 6th June, 1873; clerk, 2nd class, April, 1879; staff clerk, July, 1896; joint editor of the India List, 1885-95, and of the India Office List, 1895-96.

KEITH, THOMAS W., late Acctnt.-Gen., India Office.—Educ. at Highgate Sch.; East India house writer, old establ., 20th Aug., 1845; junr. clerk, treasury, 1848; senr. clerk, Sept., 1858; acctnt.-gen. s. dept., 1860; acctnt., Jan., 1872; assessor of income tax, 1872; acctnt.-gen., Feb., 1879; retd., June, 1893.

KEITH, WILLIAM JOHN, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma).—Educ. at Edinburgh Univ. and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived, 30th Nov., 1896, and served in Burma as asst. commr.

KELLAKKA COVILAGOM MANAVEDAN RAJA, B.A., Statutory C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Madras).—Educ. at the Madras Univ.; apptd. probr. for the civil service, 4th June, 1880; confd., Aug., 1882; asst. collr. and mag., July, 1882; head asst., Aug., 1888; sub. collr. and joint mag., Feb. 1895; dist. and sess. judge, Mar., 1897.

KELLEY, WILLIAM (second class clerk, Acctnt.-Gen. s. Dept., India Office).—Clerk, 2nd class, 9th Oct., 1879.

KELLY, CHARLES ARTHUR, M.A., late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at Oriel Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1860; arrived, 14th Nov., 1861; served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., joint mag. and dep. collr., and asst. supt. of survey; small cause court judge, Jan., 1876; district and sessions judge, Jan., 1879; retd., Jan., 1888.

KELSALL, JOHN, late Madras C.S.—Educ. at Univ. Coll. Sch., and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1862; from March, 1865, to Jan., 1867, served as proby. asst. supt. of police, and subsequently as asst. collr. and mag., and principal asst.; district and sessions judge, Aug., 1880; retd., Nov., 1889; compiled the 'Bellary District Manual.'

KEMBALL, SIR ARNOLD BURROWS, K.C.B., K.C.S.I., Gen., late Foreign Dept., Govt. of India.—Educ. at Addiscombe; 2nd lieut., Bombay artillery, Dec., 1837; served in the Afghan war, 1838-39, and was present at the siege and capture of Ghazni (medal); apptd. asst. to the rest. at Bushire, Feb., 1842; pol. rest., Persian Gulf, May, 1852; consul gen., Bagdad, and pol. agent, Turkish Arabia, 1855 to 1873; served in the Persian expdn. of 1857 (medal and clasp, C.B.); served with the Turco-Persian boundary commn., 1873; during the Russo-Turkish war, 1876-77, was British mil. attaché at the headquarters of the Turkish army.

KEMBALL, CHARLES GURDON, B.L., late Bombay C.S.—Apptd. from Haileybury in 1850; arrived, 25th Nov., 1850; served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., asst. judge and sessions judge, jud. asst. commr. (in Sind), and dep. mag.; collr. and mag., Karachi, Nov., 1861; served as judge and sessions judge, Surat, from Jan., 1866; judge of the high court, Bombay,

Aug., 1871; acted as member of council, Bombay, in 1880 and 1881; ret'd., 1885.

KEMBLE, WILLIAM, late Bengal C.S.—Apptd. from Haileybury; arrived, 8th April, 1858; served in Bengal, and was apptd. mag. and collr., Oct., 1873; inspr.-gen. of jails, July, 1876; offg. opium agent, Benares, May, 1882; opium agent, Behar, May, 1884; offg. commr., Patna, May-Nov., 1890; acting member of board of rev., June, 1892; ret'd., April, 1893.

KENDALL, EDWARD AUSTIN, Indian C.S. (joint mag., N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at the Bedford Gr. Sch., and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1890; arrived, 22nd Oct., 1892, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; judge, small cause court, July, 1896; joint mag., July, 1898.

KENNAWAY, FREDERICK WALTER, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at Dulwich and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1897; arrived 27th Dec., 1898, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

KENNEDY, HARTLEY (commr., Bombay Police).—Joined the dept., 30th Oct., 1871; asst. supt. of police, Jan., 1874; supt., March, 1876; inspr.-genl., Dec., 1896; commr., Jan., 1899.

KENNEDY, JAMES, late Bengal C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at the High Sch. and Univ. of Edinburgh; apptd. after exam. of 1862; arrived, 14th Nov., 1863, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., settl. officer, and joint mag. and dep. collr.; mag. and collr., March, 1884; ret'd., April, 1890.

KENNEDY, JOSEPH, Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at the High Sch. and Univ. of Edinburgh; apptd. after exam. of 1872; arrived, 23rd Oct., 1874, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and in Assam (1884-86) as asst. commr. and dep. commr.; mag. and collr., Bengal, Nov., 1892.

KENNEDY, ROBERT GREIG, India Public Works Dept. (supg. engr.).—Trained at Cooper's Hill Coll.; apptd., 1st Oct., 1873, and employed on irrign. works in the Punjab; exec. engr., Oct., 1881; transf. to Baluchistan, Oct., 1884; returned to the Punjab, Aug., 1890; supg. engr., Feb., 1898.

KENNEDY, ROBERT MUIRHEAD, Indian C.S. (collr. and mag., Bombay).—Educ. at Rugby; apptd. after exam. of 1875; arrived, 16th Nov., 1877, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and railway mag., Bandra; 1st asst., May, 1891; in 1891 acted as pol. agent, Mahi Kantha, and as administrator, Rajpipla state; talukdari settl. officer, Nov., 1891; on depn. to adjudicate on Cutch Morvi, &c., disputes, June, 1894-June, 1896; junr. collr. and mag., Nov., 1896; senr. collr., June, 1897; acting commr. of customs, June, 1898; actg. sec. to govr., pol. and jud. depts., February, 1899; collr. and mag., May, 1899.

KENNEDY, THOMAS JOHN, B.A., Indian C.S. (divl. judge, Punjab).—Educ. at the Liverpool Institute, Balliol Coll., Oxford, and London Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1878; arrived, 20th Nov., 1880, and served in the Punjab and Central Prov. (1885-87) as asst. commr. and settl. officer; district judge, April, 1892; dep. commr., December, 1894; divl. judge, August, 1896.

KENNEDY, WILLOUGHBY PITCAIRN, Lieut.-

Col., I.S.C., Pol. Dept., Bombay (administrator, Navanagar).—First commn., 8th Feb., 1870; from March, 1875, served as asst. to pol. agent, Mahi Kantha, Aden, Zanzibar, Baluchistan, and Kathiawar; asst. to the agent to the govr.-gen., Central India, Aug.-Oct., 1880; special pol. agent, Cambay, 1890-94; second asst. to pol. agent, Kathiawar, Sept., 1893; administrator, Rajpipla state, Oct., 1894; administrator, Navanagar state, July, 1895.

KENNEY - HERBERT, ARTHUR ROBERT, Col., late Madras Cavalry.—Cornet, 20th Sept., 1859; arrived, 31st Oct., 1859; mil. sec. to the govr. of Madras, May, 1881, to April, 1884; offg. asst. sec. to govt. of India, mil. dept., Dec., 1881; sec. to the govt. of Madras, mil. dept., Dec., 1885; ret'd., April, 1892.

KENSINGTON, ALFRED, B.A., Indian C.S. (divl. judge, Punjab).—Educ. at Marlborough, and Univ. Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1875; arrived, 12th Feb., 1878, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr., asst. settl. officer, and offg. under sec.; settl. officer, April, 1884; on special duty in Ambala settl., Dec., 1889; under sec. to govt. of India, fin. and commerce dept., April, 1890; retransf. to Punjab as dep. commr., Dec., 1894; divl. judge, March, 1896; transf. to N.W.P. and Oudh as acctnt.-gen., class III, June, 1896; returned to Punjab as divl. judge, Nov., 1897.

KER, THOMAS, M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. asst. engr., 16th May, 1874, and employed on state railways in Rajputana; exec. engr., May, 1883; ret'd., May, 1894.

KERNAN, JAMES, M.A., Q.C., late Jud. Dept., Madras.—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Dublin; judge of the high court, Madras, 3rd May, 1870; fellow of Madras univ., 1871; vice-chancellor, 1885 and 1887; offd. as chief justice, 1885 and 1888; ret'd., July, 1889.

KERR, JOHN HENRY, Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Glasgow Acad., Glasgow Univ., and Clare Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1890; arrived, 29th Nov., 1892, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

KERR, THOMAS COULTER, Capt., Store Dept., India Office (surveyor of shipping).—Educ. at Liverpool Coll.; served during China war, 1860-61, and Abyssinian war, 1868-69, in command of hired troopers; surveyor of shipping, India office, 1st April, 1881.

KERSHASP, PIROZESHAH, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Madras).—Educ. at Bombay University and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1894; arrived 20th Dec., 1895, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.

KERSHAW, LOUIS JAMES, Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Assam).—Educ. at Belfast Methodist Coll. and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1888; arrived, 31st Oct., 1890, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transf. to Assam as asst. commr., April, 1892; dep. commr., April, 1898.

KETTLEWELL, ARTHUR BRADLEY, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at Cheltenham Coll., and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1890; arrived, 31st Oct., 1892, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

KEYS, ARTHUR INNES (clerk, Indo-European Telegraph Dept., India Office).—Apptd. clerk,

London office, Indo-European telegraph dept., 7th Feb., 1880; transfed. to registry and record dept., India office, Jan., 1889; clerk specially attached, Aug., 1891; rejoined Indo-European telegraph dept., June, 1894.

KEYSER, ALFRED, late Bombay C.S.—Educ. at Guildford Gr. Sch., and Royal Acad., Gosport; apptd. after exam. of 1864; arrived, 29th Oct., 1865, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; junr. collr. and mag., and pol. agent, Surat, Dec., 1884; senr. collr., &c., April, 1887; acting commr., S. divn., March, 1890; ret'd., March, 1892.

KHANDRAO CHIMANRAO VEDARKAR, Rao Bahadur, B.A., LL.B., late Jud. Dept., Bombay.—Joined the service, 25th April, 1863, as Gujarati and Marathi translr. and interpreter, high court; dep. registrar and sealer, Feb., 1867; services lent to the Maharaja Holkar, Oct., 1890; judge, Bombay small cause court, Dec., 1890, continuing to be in foreign service; ret'd., Jan., 1896.

KHANDUBHAI GULABHAI DESAI, L.C.E., Bombay Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Joined the service, 11th April, 1870, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., April, 1884; is a fellow of the Bombay univ.

KHARSEDJI MANEKJI KHARSEDJI, Khan Bahadur, B.A., B.L. (judge, small cause court, Bombay).—Served from 29th May, 1874, as judge of the court of small causes, Ahmadnagar, and subsequently at Ahmadabad; judge, Bombay small cause court, June, 1888.

KHARSEDJI RATANJI BAMANJI, Statutory C.S. (forest settlt. offr., Bombay).—Probr. under the native civil service rules, and asst. collr. and mag., Nov., 1882; confd., 8th Jan., 1885; 1st asst., Jan., 1895; forest settlt. offr., April, 1896.

KHUSHWAKT RAE, R.A.F., B.L., N.W. Prov. Administration (dep. collr.).—Apptd. proby. asst. mag. and collr., under the native civil service rules, 8th April, 1890; dep. collr., April, 1894.

KIERNANDER, CHARLES ROBERT CAMPBELL, late Finl. Dept., Govt. of India.—Joined the dept. 5th Aug., 1864, and served as asst. and dep. acctnt.-gen., inspr. of local offices of acct., and dep. comptr.-gen.; asst. sec., govt. of India, finl. dept., 1884-85; comptr. of Indian treasuries, April, 1885; dep. auditor-gen., Oct., 1886; dep. comptr.-gen., April, 1891; ret'd., March, 1892.

KILBY, GEORGE CHARLES, B.L., late Bengal Jud. Service.—Dep. supt. and rememberer of legal affairs, 28th Feb., 1876; ret'd., Sept., 1896.

KILBY, REGINALD GEORGE, Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Winchester and Pembroke Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived, 7th Dec., 1896, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

KILBY, SANFORD JAMES, late Bengal Preventive Service.—Asst. collr. of customs, Chittagong, 30th June, 1865; dep. mag. and dep. collr., July, 1866; from Aug., 1868, served in the police dept.; on famine relief duty, 1873 and 1876; from Dec., 1876, served in the customs preventive service, and also in Madras as dep. commr., abbkari and salt dept.; ret'd., Nov., 1892.

KILGOUR, FREDERICK, Col., I.S.C., late Police Dept., Madras.—First commn., 11th July,

1857; proby. asst. supt. of police, Nov., 1865; asst. supt., Aug., 1873; supt., June, 1874; dep. inspr.-gen., May, 1892; reverted to mil. dept., July, 1895.

KIMBERLEY (EARL OF), RIGHT HON. JOHN WODEHOUSE, K.G., P.C., D.C.L.—Born, 7th Jan., 1826; educ. at Eton, and Christ Church, Oxford; succeeded to title of Baron Wodehouse, May, 1846; under sec. of state for foreign affairs, 1852-56; ambassador at St. Petersburg, 1856-58; under sec. for foreign affairs, June, 1859, to Aug., 1861; on a special mission to the North of Europe, in connection with the Schleswig-Holstein question, 1863; under sec. of state for India, April-Nov., 1864; lord lieut. of Ireland; 1864-66; raised to earldom of Kimberley, 1866; elected govr. of the Hudson's Bay Co., 1868; lord privy seal, 1868-70; sec. of state for the colonies, 1870-74; member of the royal commn. on the penal servitude acts, Feb., 1878; sec. of state for the colonies, May, 1880; held provisionally the office of chancellor of the duchy of Lancaster, on Mr. Bright's resignation, June, 1882; sec. of state for India, Dec., 1882, to June, 1885, and Feb. to Aug., 1886; again sec. of state for India, and also lord prest. of the council, Aug., 1892; sec. of state for foreign affairs, March, 1894, to July, 1895; K.G., 1885; hon. D.C.L. (Oxford), June, 1894; was a royal commr. for the exhibn. of 1886; was for some time prest. of Univ. Coll., London (resigned, 1887).

KINCAID, CHARLES AUGUSTUS, Indian C.S. (2nd asst., Bombay).—Educ. at Sherborne Sch., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1889; arrived, 23rd Nov., 1891, and served in Sind as asst. collr. and mag.; 2nd asst., July, 1897.

KINCAID, WILLIAM, Major-Gen., I.S.C., late Foreign Dept., Govt. of India.—Ensign, Madras army, March, 1849; apptd. to adjust boundaries disputes, Bhopal agency, March, 1864; asst. pol. agent, Bundelkhand, and small cause court judge and cantonmt. mag., Nowgong, 1866; pol. agent, Bhopal, Aug., 1876; pol. agent, Bhopawar, and commandant, Malwa Bhil corps, Oct., 1879; pol. agent, Bhopal, June, 1881; returned to Europe, 1887.

KINDERSLEY, JOHN ROBERT, late Madras C.S.—Apptd. from Haileybury in 1847; served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr. till 1857, when he was apptd. asst. judge of Mangalore; subord. judge, 1859; civil and sessions judge, March, 1865; offg. jud. commr., Mysore, 1866 and 1868; judge of the high court, Madras, June, 1877; ret'd., 1884.

KING, CHARLES MONTAGUE, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1890; arrived, 10th Dec., 1892, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

KING, SIR GEORGE, K.C.I.E., M.B., LL.D., F.R.S., Brigade-Surgn.-Lieut.-Col., late Bengal Med. Service.—First commn., 1865; house surgn., med. coll. hosp., Calcutta, 1866; supt. of royal botanical gardens, profr. of botany in Calcutta med. coll., and supt. of cinchona plantation in British Sikhimm, from July, 1871; also quinelogist, Dec., 1879; C.I.E., Jan., 1890; in addn., dir. of the botanical survey of India, May, 1891; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1898; ret'd., Feb., 1898; author of 'Manual of Cinchona Cultivation in India,' and other botanical works; and editor of

'Annals of the Royal Botanical Gardens, Calcutta.'

KING, JOSHUA, M.A., late Bombay C.S.—Educ. at Trinity Hall, Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1859; arrived, 26th Oct., 1860, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and acting under sec. to govt.; talukdari settlt. officer, 1874-76 and 1876-77; on special duty, Baroda, 1877-79; junr. collr. and mag., Ahmadnagar, May, 1879; senr. collr. and mag., and pol. agent, Satara, May, 1884; ret'd., 1886.

KING, LUCAS BLACKER BURNET, M.A., late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1859; arrived, 11th Oct., 1860; from Nov., 1861, served in Bengal in various grades, and as supt. of survey, and district and sessions judge; ret'd., August, 1885.

KING, LUCAS WHITE, C.S.I., B.A., LL.D., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at Ennis Coll., and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1876; arrived, 27th Nov., 1878, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; transf'd. to foreign dept. as offg. asst. commr., Ajmer, June, 1885; pol. agent, Oct., 1885; asst. to rest., Mysore, and sec. to chief commr., Coorg, July, 1887; on special duty with Maharaja of Mysore, Dec., 1887; first asst. to agent for Central India, Feb.-July, 1888; retransf'd. to Punjab as asst. commr., Sept., 1890; also pol. officer; Shirani field force, Oct., 1890; dep. commr., Nov., 1893; on special duty with Waziristan delimitation commn., 1894-95; pol. officer with Tirah expedit. force, Oct., 1897—Jan., 1898; C.S.I., May, 1898; author of a history of the Barakzai coinage of Afghanistan.

KING, MAURICE, M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Trained at Calcutta Civil Engrg. Coll.; apptd., 20th Jan., 1868, and employed on irrign. works in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh; exec. engr., April, 1876; transf'd. to Burma as suptg. engr., May, 1893; re-transf'd. to N.W. Prov., Nov., 1896; ret'd., March, 1898.

KING, WALTER GAWEN, C.I.E., M.B., C.M., D.P.H., Lieut.-Col., Indian Med. Service (sanitary commr., Madras).—Arrived, 31st Oct., 1874, and served under the mil. dept. in Madras and Burma; apptd. civil surgn., June, 1877; also supt. of jail, March, 1878; reverted to mil. dept., Jan., 1892; acting supt., Madras lunatic asylum, April, 1893; sanitary commr., June, 1895; C.I.E., June, 1899.

KING, WILLIAM, B.A., D.Sc., late Geological Survey of India.—Educ. at Queen's Coll., Galway; apptd. asst. in the geological survey, 4th March, 1857; dep. supt. for Madras, Nov., 1868; served subsequently in the Godavari district and the Nizam's territories, and discovered the Singareni coal-field, 1871-72; depd. to Vienna in joint charge of the survey collections at the exhibn., 1873; fellow of Madras univ., 1884; offd. as supt., 1884; apptd. dir., April, 1887; ret'd., July, 1894; author of contributions to the survey publns.

KING, WILLIAM HENRY, M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. asst. engr., 21st Dec., 1868, and posted to Bengal; transf'd. to mil. works, April, 1872; exec. engr., Sept., 1879; retransf'd. to Bengal, 1879; employed in Maluchristan, 1885-88; and acted as joint sec. to govr.-gen.'s agent; ret'd., Sept., 1894.

KING-HARMAN, MONTAGUE JOCELYN, Col.,

I.S.C., late Mil. Secretariat, Govt. of India.—First commn., 2nd Oct., 1860; served in Hazara campaign, 1868 (medal), and Lushai expdn., 1871-72; dep. asst. quartermaster-gen., Bengal, 1877; dep. sec., mil. dept., govt. of India, Aug., 1892 till Aug., 1897.

KINGSFORD, DOUGLAS HOLLINSHEAD, Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Haileybury Coll., and Trinity Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1893; arrived, 28th Dec., 1894, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr. and asst. settlt. officer.

KINGSMILL, JAMES, late Central Press, Bombay.—Gen. supt. of the central press, and editor of the 'Bombay Govt. Gazette,' March, 1872-1891.

KINSMAN, FREDERICK, B.Sc. (chief supt., India Telegraph Dept.).—Apptd. asst. supt., 24th Feb., 1870; supt., Sept., 1886; personal supt. to dir.-gen., Feb.-May, 1888; in charge, Upper Burma divn., May, 1893; chief supt., March, 1897.

KIPLING, JOHN LOCKWOOD, C.I.E., late Educn. Dept., Punjab.—Joined the govt. service as architectural sculptor, Bombay, May, 1865; from April, 1875, served in the Punjab as principal of Mayo sch. of industrial art, and curator, Lahore museum; ret'd., May, 1893.

KIRK, HENRY ALEXANDER (chief supt., India Telegraph Dept.).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 24th Feb., 1870; supt., March, 1876; served during the Afghan war, 1879-80; in charge, telegraph workshops, Calcutta, Oct., 1885; chief supt., Sept., 1896.

KIRKHAM, THOMAS BROWN, B.L., late Educn. Dept., Bombay.—Joined the dept., 1st Jan., 1862; arrived, 21st June, 1862, and served as head-master of the Poona high sch., principal of the Elphinstone high sch., Bombay, and profir. at the Elphinstone coll. ; from April, 1878, served as educ. inspr.; addl. member of Bombay legisl. council, July 1895; ret'd., Oct., 1898.

KIRKPATRICK, HENRY CLARE (reporter, Bombay high court).—Joined the service 8th Jan., 1878, and served as reporter to the high court.

KIRKWOOD, TOWNSEND MOLLOY, late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at Bromsgrove Gr. Sch.; apptd. after exam. of 1862; arrived, 2nd Dec., 1863, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., joint mag. and dep. collr., and district and sessions judge; also in the public works dept., 1869-71, and was on famine duty, Bhagalpur, 1874; ret'd., 1888.

KIRTON, WILLIAM FERDINAND, Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Queen's Coll., Trinidad, Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford, and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1893; arrived, 10th Dec., 1894, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr. and asst. commr.

KISCH, HERMANN MICHAEL, M.A., B.L., Indian C.S. (dep. dir.-gen. of post office).—Educ. at Univ. Coll., London, and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1871; arrived, 24th Oct., 1873; asst. mag. and collr., Bengal, Nov., 1873; on famine duty in 1874 and 1877; asst. to dir.-gen. of statistics, Feb., 1875; offg. under sec., govt. of Bengal, rev. and gen. depts., May, 1878; under sec., jud. and pol. depts., Jan., 1880; offg. sec. in the finl. dept., June, 1881; offg. under sec., govt. of India, rev. and agricl. dept., Dec., 1883; postmr.-gen., Bengal, Nov., 1885; offd. as dep.

sec., fin. and commerce dept., in 1889, and as dir. gen. of post office of India in 1890-91 and 1893-95; on depn. to Vienna postal congress, 1891; dep. dir.-gen. of post office, Feb., 1897; on depn. to Washington postal congress, 1897; author of statistical accts. of Chittagong and other districts.

KISSORY MOHUN CHATTERJEE, B.A., B.L. (judge, Calcutta small cause court).—Offd. as judge of Calcutta small cause court in 1888 and 1889, and was confd. in that post, 3rd Oct., 1889; author of a legal treatise on the transfer of immovable property.

KITCHIN, ARTHUR JAMES WARBURTON, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at Clifton Coll., and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1892; arrived, 4th Jan., 1894, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

KITTS, EUSTACE JOHN, Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Portland Gr. Sch., Mill Hill Gr. Sch., and Univ. of London; apptd. after exam. of 1872; arrived, 29th Oct., 1874, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., and in Berar as asst. commr. and small cause court judge; on famine duty in Madras, 1877-78; asst. commr., Ajmer, 1883; from April, 1884, served in the N.W. Prov. as joint mag., asst. commr., and small cause court judge; district and sessions judge, Dec., 1890; author of 'Berar Census Report' (1882), and 'Castes and Tribes found in India' (1885).

KNAPP, ARTHUR ROWLAND, Indian C.S. (under sec. to govt., Madras).—Educ. at Westminster, and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1889; arrived, 20th Nov., 1891, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; under sec. to govt., Feb., 1899.

KNIGHT, RAYMONT, Indian C.S. (judge and sess. judge, Bombay).—Educ. at St. Paul's Coll., Stony Stratford, and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1882; arrived, 12th Jan., 1885, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and forest settlt. officer; 1st asst., May, 1896; judge and sessions judge, Feb., 1897.

KNOWLYS, HENRY BOLD, late Berar Commn.—Served as a writer in the Ceylon civil service, Feb., 1859, to Aug., 1866; from Sept., 1866, served in the Haidarabad assigned districts as asst. commr.; small cause court judge, Sept., 1873; dep. commr., May, 1886; ret'd., July, 1891.

KNOX, GEORGE, late Punjab Administration.—Asst. engr., May, 1854; from June, 1856, served in the Punjab commn. as personal asst. to commr., dep. commr., and offg. commr.; ret'd., 1890.

KNOX, GEORGE EDWARD, Indian C.S. (Judge, high court, N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Merchant Taylors' Sch. and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1864; arrived, 21st Nov., 1865, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; from March, 1877, served in the jud. dept. as small cause court judge, and district and sessions judge; member of the provl. legisl. council, 1886 and 1889; legal remembrancer, Sept., 1887; judge of the high court, N.W. Prov., Dec., 1890; author of a 'Digest of the Criminal Law of the Bengal Presidency,' a 'Digest of Civil Procedure,' 'The Legal Remembrancer,' 1879-82, and 'Treatise upon the Indian Stamp Law.'

KNOX, HORACE CHALONER, M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Trained at Cooper's

Hill Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1875, and posted to state railways; exec. engr., May, 1885; ret'd., 1898.

KNOX, HUBERT THOMAS, B.L., late Madras C.S.—Apptd. after exam. of 1866; arrived 4th Dec., 1868, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr., joint sessions judge for trying Rumpu cases, and asst. commr. (Nilgiris) sub-collr. and joint mag., Oct., 1884; district and sessions judge, Nov., 1887; collr. and mag., Nov., 1888; ret'd., Jan., 1892.

KNYVETT, ALEXANDER VANSITTART (asst. to inspr.-gen. of police, Bengal).—Joined the service, 1st Jan., 1867, as asst. supt. of police; district supt., Dec., 1876; personal asst. to the inspr.-gen. of police, May, 1892.

KNYVETT, CHARLES FREDERICK (dep. inspr.-gen. of police, N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Joined the service, 25th June, 1869, as acting asst. district supt.; confd., Oct., 1873; district supt., Dec., 1878; dep. inspr.-gen. of police, Jan., 1895.

KNYVETT, WILLIAM LEYCESTER NATHANIEL, Col., I.S.C., late Police Dept., Bengal.—First commn., 10th June, 1854; served in the dept. from June, 1863, as district supt.; dep. inspr.-gen., April, 1885; offg. inspr.-gen., July, 1888; reverted to mil. duty, 1891.

KONSTAM, EDWIN MAX, Indian C.S. (joint mag. and dep. collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Marlborough, and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1888; arrived, 29th Nov., 1890, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; under sec. to govt., Feb., 1896; joint mag. and dep. collr., July, 1898.

COUGH, CHARLES, B.A., late Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at Kingstown, Dungannon, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1865; arrived, 16th Dec., 1867, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag., head asst., and sub-collr. and joint mag.; forest settlt. officer, March, 1884; collr. and mag., Nov., 1887; ret'd., July, 1894.

KRISHNA CHUNDER CHATTERJEE, B.A., B.L., late Jud. Dept., Bengal.—From 19th Dec., 1865, served in the subord. jud. service, Bengal, and was apptd. subord. judge, May, 1877.

KRISHNA GOVINDA GUPTA, B.L., Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Maimansingh Zilla Sch., Pogose Sch., Dacca, and Calcutta Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1871; arrived, 24th Oct., 1873, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; on famine duty, 1874; joint mag. and dep. collr., Sept., 1884; mag. and collr., Oct., 1889; junr. sec. to the board of rev., May, 1890; commr. of excise, Bengal, March, 1893; mag. and collr., March, 1895.

KUCHLER, GEORGE WILLIAM, B.A. (prof., Presy. Coll., Calcutta).—Apptd. profir., presy. coll., Calcutta, March, 1885; has held professorships at Patna Coll., and at the Sibpur Enggr. Coll.

KYNOCH, GEORGE COLLINGWOOD (chief collr. of customs, Burma).—Joined the service, June, 1877, and served as asst. sec. to chief commr.; chief collr. of customs, April, 1896.

LAING, JAMES ANDERSON, M.D., Surgn.-Lieut.-Col., late Madras Med. Dept.—Asst. surgn., 1st April, 1870; arrived, 26th Dec., 1870, and served as civil surgn. and supt. of jail, and acting rest. surgn. at the gen. hosp., Madras; dep. sanitary commr. and inspr. of

vacn., Feb., 1878; commr., June, 1888; retd., Sept., 1895.
